Anby, John, s. J., of Sherwood, par. Killington, Yorks, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 May, 1682, aged 15; B.A. 28 Jan., 1685-6, M.A. 22 March, 1688-9, rector of Thornhill, Yorks, 1701. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Dale, Hugh, s. William, of Cholmley Hall, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 July, 1682, aged 18; B.A. 1686, M.A. 22 March, 1688-9, rector of Middle, Salop, 1689. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Gregson, William, s. Rob., of Plumton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1682, aged 18; B.A. 28 Jan., 1685-6, vicar of Melling, co. Lancaster, 1695. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Blagge, Jonathan, s. William, of Macclesfield, Cheshire, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1682-3, aged 18; B.A. 1686, rector of Harescombe with Pitchcombe, co. Gloucester, 1708.
Hancox, John, s. Tho., of Hereford city, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 July, 1682, aged 16, B.A. 1686; one of these names rector of Foots Cray, Kent, 1691. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Adams, William, s. William, of Longdon, Salop, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 May, 1682, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 24 May, 1691. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Nanney, William, s. Griffin, of Dolgelly, co. Merioneth, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 Nov., 1682, aged 18, rector of Horton, Bucks, 1695. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Baxter, Alexander, s. William, of Rushall, Wilts, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 April, 1682, aged 16; B.A. 1685, M.A. 1688, his father rector of Rushall 1653.
Cocks, Thomas, s. Th., of Eastnor, co. Hereford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 July, 1682, aged 16; B.A. 1686, M.A. 1691, brother of John 1679.
Cooper, Peter, s. Nath., of Runcorn, co. Chester, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1682-3, aged 18; B.A. 1686, brother of Nathl. 1680.
Baskervyle, John, s. Tho., of Presbury, co. Chester, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1682, aged 18. See Ormerod's Cheshire, iii. 718.
Browne, John, s. Rob., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 March, 1682-3, aged 18; B.A. 1686.
Drinkwater, William, s. Arnold, of Warburton, co. Chester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 March, 1682-3, aged 16.
Easton, Thomas, s. John, of Batsford, co. Gloucester, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 Dec., 1682. aged 14.
Silston, Charles, s. John, of Lea, co. Lincoln, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1682-3, aged 16.
Parkinson, Adam, s. Tho., of Leigh, co. Lancaster, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 July, 1682, aged 17.
Wilcox, John, s. J., of Mainston, Salop, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1682, aged 24.

Wake, Robert, youngest son of William, of Piddington, Northants, bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 March, 1683-4, scholar Trinity Coll. 1685, B.A. 1687, M.A. 1690, fellow 1693; vicar of Fritwell, Oxon, 1690-1703, rector of Farthingstone, Northants, 1714-20, and of Bocking, Essex, 1724-5; rector of Buxted with chapelry of Cuckfield, Sussex, and vicar of Ogbourne St. Andrew, Wilts. See Rawl. i. 190, v. 174, and xxi. 165; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Broade, Francis, s. Thomas, of Mordiford, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4; B.A. 1687, M.A. 1690, vicar of Brinsop, co. Hereford, 1691, and of Yazor 1692, canon of Hereford 1702, rector of Stoke Lacy 1706, and of Bromyard (2nd portion) 1709. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bunbury, William, s. William, of Stoke, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4; B.A. 1687, fellow 1687, M.A. 1690 (incorp. at Cambridge 1693), B.D. 1705, rector of Catworth, Hunts, 1704, born at Stanney, Cheshire, 18 May, 1667. See Rawlinson, xvi. 337.

Walker, John, of co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 May, 1683, aged 18, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1693, as son and heir of William, of Priesthall, near Kirkham, co. Lancashire, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Greathed, Edward, s. E., of Nottingham, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 1687; perhaps rector of West Barkwith, co. Lincoln, 1713. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hunter, Samuel, of Cheshire, cler. fil. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 May, 1683, aged 17; B.A. 18 Jan., 1686-7; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 1689, rector of Leaden Roding, Essex, 1699. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Newton, Peter, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 March, 1683-4; B.A. from Hart Hall 1687; M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1701; rector of Lydham, Salop, 1689. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bolton, Robert, s. Jos., of Wigan, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 March, 1683-4, aged 17; fellow Corpus Christi Coll., B.A. 1687, M.A. 1691, admon. at Oxford, granted 4 Nov., 1699.
Shuttleworth, Barton, s. B., of Blackburn, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1683, aged 18; student of Gray's Inn 1684; ? father of Barton 1726. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Fleetwood, Henry, s. Arthur, of Lichfield, co. Stafford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 Oct., 1683, aged 16; succeeded to Penwortham estate, co. Lancaster, in 1704, but it was sold in 1749.
Oakeley, Richard, s. R., of Broadstow, Salop, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 March, 1683, aged 18; one of these names rector of Cold Weston, Salop, 1706. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jenkinson, Simeon, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 July, 1683, aged 16; one of these names vicar of Flintham, Notts, 1695. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Mallowes, Benjamin, s. William, of Dinder, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4, B.A. 1687, M.A. 1690.
Berkeley, Charles (Barkley), s. William, of Chester (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1683, aged 18; B.A. 1686.
Shaw, Francis, s. Thom., of Eccles, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4, B.A. 1687, M.A. 1690.
Sharples, James, s. Geo., of Kyrkham, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1683, aged 16, B.A. 1686.
Goodwin, John, s. Matth., of Bloxham, Oxon, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 March, 1683-4, B.A. 1687.
Deane, Henry, s. Peter, of Rotherston, co. Chester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4.
Savory, Peter, s. P., of Warrington, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4.
Hall, Edward, s. Mich., of Rengh-Abby, co. Denbigh, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4.
Houghton, Matthew, of co. Lancaster, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 July, 1683, aged 16.
Dod, John, s. J., of Chittingstone, Kent, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1683-4.
Smethurst, Thomas, of Yorks, cler. fil. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 July, 1683, aged 20.
Farrington, John, of Cheshire, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 June, 1683, aged 17.
Hunt, George, of Oxon, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 May, 1683, aged 20.

Bedford, Arthur, s. Richard, of Tiddenham, co. Gloucester, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 May, 1684, aged 15; B.A. 23 Feb., 1687-8, M.A. 9 July, 1691, baptized at Tuddenham 8 Sept., 1668, vicar of Temple Church, Bristol, 1692, rector of Newton St. Loe, Somerset, 1713, chaplain of Aske's Hospital, Hoxton, lecturer of St. Botolph's, Aldgate, chaplain to Frederick Prince of Wales. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Hearne, i. 13; Rawlinson, xvi. 160; & D.N.B.
Savage, Robert, s. Tho., of Worcester (city), p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 July, 1684, aged 16; B.A. 1688; rector of Walton-on-the-Hill, Surrey, 1690. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lindley, Francis, s. Fr., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 May, 1684, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1692. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Rode, Christopher, s. James, of Ashbury, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 July, 1684, aged 17; perhaps father of William 1707.
Wilkinson, Thomas, s. Christopher, of London, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 July, 1684, aged 19, B.A. 1689; see also next man.
Sorocold, Michael, s. James, of Leigh, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1684-5, aged 17, B.A. 1688.
Walworth, Thomas, s. T., of Clifton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1684-5, aged 19, B.A. 1688.
Kearsley, James, s. J., of Dean, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1684, aged 19; B.A. 1688.
Rome, William, s. G. (? "Gul."), of Northampton (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1684, aged 16.
Toulson, Clemens, s. Geo., of Stakes, Yorks, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 July, 1684, aged 16.

Coleire, Richard, s. Walter, of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Sept., 1685, aged 15; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1688, B.A. 1689, M.A. 1693, proctor 1701, chaplain to Colonel Wyndham's regiment 1696, chaplain to Viscount Falkland and to Marchioness Dowager of Wharton, rector of Harrietsham, Kent, 1702-16, vicar of Tong, Kent, 1712, and of Isleworth, Middlesex, 1716, and of Kingstonupon-Thames 1720-6, minister of Richmond, Surrey, 1726. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Rawlinson, i. 107, iv. 116, xvi. 394; & Hearne, i. 230.

Tilly, Samuel, s. S., of Martock, Somerset, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 Jan., 1685-6, aged 16, scholar Wadham Coll. 1687, B.A. 1689, M.A. 1692, fellow 1694-1702, B.D. 1712; admitted to Merchant Taylors' school 1675, born July, 1666; rector of Eccleston, Cheshire, 1701-4, and of Bladon, Oxon, 1704, and domestic chaplain to the earl of Abingdon; died 24 Aug., 1712, buried 26 in St. Mary's, Oxford; brother of William 1689. See Robinson, i. 285; Gardiner, 350; & Hearne, iii. 437.

Harrison, Joseph, s. R(ichard), of Brindle, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1685, aged 15; B.A. 1689, M.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1702; perpetual curate Cirencester 1690, chancellor of the diocese and commissary of the dean and chapter of Canterbury, rector of Daglingworth, co. Gloucester, 1729; father of John 1713. See Rawl. iv. 346, xvii. 209; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Mosley, Francis, s. Fr., of Wilmslow, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 April, 1685, aged 16, B.A. 1688; M.A. from Merton Coll. 1691; rector of Rolleston, co. Stafford, 1693; (his father vicar of Bowden 1667, and rector of Wilmslow, Cheshire, 1674, etc.); died 1738. See Foster's Lancashire Collection & Foster's Index Eccl.

Thompson, William, s. William, of Chester (city), cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 March, 1685-6, aged 16, B.A. 1689, fellow, M.A. 1692, proctor 1700, B.D. 1705, D.D. 1709, vice-principal; died 4 April, 1713, buried in the college chapel, admon. at Oxford 18th. See Gutch, i. 375; & Hearne, ii. 18.

Buerdsell, James, s. J., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 April, 1685, aged 15; B.A. 1688, fellow, M.A. 1692, died 2 Oct., 1700, aged 30, buried in Brasenose cloisters, admon. (at Oxford) granted 4 Oct. See Hearne, i. 98; Rawlinson, i. 427; & Gutch, i. 377.

Usherwood, John, s. Tho., of Eccles, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 April, 1685, aged 18, B.A. 1688; M.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1693; rector of Wilmslow, Cheshire, 1699, until his death 3 Oct., 1705. See Earwaker, i. 93; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Yate, Thomas, s. P., of Middlewich, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1685, aged 18, B.A. 1689, M.A. 1692; curate to Dr. Thomas Yate, at Middleton Cheney, Northants; died 4 Jan., 1681-2, buried in the college chapel. See Gutch, i. 381.

Bowyer, George, s. 'Jonae,' of Sandbach, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 Feb., 1685-6, aged 18; B.A. 1689, M.A. 1693, rector of Sambourne, Essex, 1715, died in March, 1740. See Rawlinson, xx. 73; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bedford, Henry, s. Edw., of Prestbury, co. Gloucester, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 April, 1685, aged 15; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 1688, M.A. 1692, rector of Little Sodbury, co. Gloucester, 1696. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Davison, Samuel, born in co. Galway ('Hibernii de Gallovidia'), s. George, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1685-6, aged 19; of the Middle Temple 1685, his father described as of Brand, Salop. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Foster, Josiah, s. J., of "Bymicham," Warwick, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 May, 1685, aged 16, B.A. 23 Jan., 1688-9; M.A. from Pembroke Hall, Cambridge, 1692, vicar of Aston-juxta-Birmingham 1716. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stanhope, Thomas, s. T(homas), of Leicester (city), S.T.D. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1685, aged 17; his father rector of St. Margaret, Leicester, 1669, and canon of Lincoln 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Gamull, William, s. William, of St. Warebert (? Warburton, St. Werberg), Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 May, 1685, aged 19; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1691. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Sneyd, Ralph, s. William, of Burches, co. Stafford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1685-6, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1685; father of Edward and Ralph. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Jones, John, s. Brainton, of Hereford city, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1685, aged 18; B.A. 1688, rector of Monnington-on-Wye, co. Hereford, 1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wilkinson, Thomas, s. T., of Whetnall, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1685, aged 16; perhaps B.A. 1689.
Astley, John, s. James, of Blackpr., co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 Feb., 1685-6, aged 18; B.A. 1689.
Cockson, Henry, s. Edw., of Steeple Barton, Oxon, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1685-6, aged 18; B.A. 1689.
Wotton, Andrew, s. Ph., of Walford, co. Stafford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 April, 1685, aged 16, B.A. 1689.
Dauntesey, William, s. Jo., of Eccles, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1685-6, aged 17.
Delahay, Robert, s. R. of Pilkington, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1685, aged 17.
Powell, John, s. Ber., of Westram, Kent, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 Feb., 1685-6, aged 18; B.A. 1689.
King, Nathaniel, s. N., of Chustock, co. Montgom., gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1685, aged 19.
Johnson, Henry, s. William, of Kingston, Surrey, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1685, aged 16.
Barker, Thomas, of Preston, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 April, 1685, aged 19.

Shippen, Edward (Sheppen), s. William, of Prestbury, Cheshire, S.T.D. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1686-7, aged 16, B.A. 1690, M.A. 1693, B. and D.Med. 1699, baptised at Prestbury 7 March, 1670-1, practised in London, professor of music at Gresham college 1710, until his death 2 Jan., 1723-4. See Rawl. i. 361.

Wodhull, Michael, s. John, of Thenford, Northants, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1686, aged 16; of Thenford, baptised there 15 May, 1670; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1696; died 11 Jan., 1738-9; brother of Charles and Thomas. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Tyler, Solomon, s. John, of Shobdon, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1686, aged 15, B.A. 1690, M.A. 1693, B. and D.D. 1704; rector of Hope Bowdler, Salop, 1694, vicar of Shobdon 1696, and of Birley, co. Hereford, 1706. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Heyes, Thomas, s. William, of Sandbach, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 May, 1686, aged 16; B.A. 11 Feb., 1689-90, M.A. 1693, B.D. 1705 (as Hayes); perhaps vicar of Old, Northants, 1706-46; his father vicar of Sandbach 1674. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hartley, William, s. Ric., of Burnley, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1686, aged 17, B.A. 1690; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 1694, vicar of Sandford, Oxon, 1695. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Le(i)gh, John, s. Thomas, of Adlington, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1686-7, aged 18; of Adlington, M.P. Bodmin, 1715, said to have died in 1735. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Weaver, Edmund, s. E., of Hereford (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 March, 1686, aged 16, B.A. 1689, M.A. 1692; vicar of Clifton-uponTeme, co. Worcester, 1698. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Cronkshaw, Nathan, s. Rob., of Padiham, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1686, aged 16; B.A. 1691, vicar of Witham-onthe Hill, co. Lincoln, 1708. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wotton, Richard, s. William, of Cholsey, Berks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 May, 1686, aged 16, B.A. from Merton Coll. 1690, M.A. 1693; brother of Benjamin, etc.
Heyes, William, s. G. (? "Gul."), of Mobberley, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 May, 1686, aged 17; B.A. 1 March, 1689-90. See also Wm. Hey, 1662.
Halliwell, John, s. Edm., of Leeds, Yorks, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1686-7, aged 15; B.A. 1690, M.A. 1693.
Wotton, John, s. Th., of Byford, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 May, 1686, aged 17.
Nott, Edward, s. E., of Liddiard, Wilts, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 May, 1686, aged 19.

Shippen, William, s. William, of Prestbury, Cheshire, S.T.D. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 July, 1687, aged 14; baptised at Prestbury 30 July, 1673, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1694-5, from Westminster school; bar.-at-law Middle Temple, 1693, M.P. Bramber Dec., 1707, until unseated Jan., 1709-10, Dec. 1710-13, Saltash 1713-15, and Newton, co. Lancaster, in 5 parliaments 1715, until his death 1 May, 1743; buried in St. Andrew, Holborn. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Martin, Amos, s. Thomas, of Crewkerne, Somerset, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 June, 1687, aged 15, B.A. 1691; one of these names student of Lincoln's Inn 1690 (as son and heir of Amos, of London, gent.). See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Orton, George, s. Uriel, of Malpas, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1687, aged 18; B.A. 1691, M.A. 1694, vicar of Preston 1693, rector of Ashington 1705, and of Slaugham 1710, all Sussex. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Booth, John, s. George, of Chester (city), arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 July, 1687, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1695, bencher 1718. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Skeate, John, s. J., of Wootton Bassett, Wilts, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 June, 1687, aged 16; drowned 8 July, 1689, buried 10th in the college chapel. See Gutch, i. 379.
Mellish, George, s. Henry, of Sanderstead, Surrey, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 June, 1687, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1687. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Parker, Alexander, s. Christopher, of Kirkham, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 June, 1687, aged 17, B.A. 1691; brother of Anthony 1674.
Martin, Thomas, s. Richard, of Kirkham, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 June, 1687, aged 17; B.A. from Hart Hall 1692.
Woods, Richard, s. Paul, of Acton, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 April, 1687, aged 15, B.A. 19 Jan., 1691-2.
Sagar, Stephen, s. William, of Colne, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 March, 1687-8, aged 18.
Tucker, Charles, s. Nic., of Hereford (town), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 April, 1687, aged 17.

Cowper, John, s. Th(omas), of Chester (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 Jan., 1688-9. aged 16; B.A. 19 Jan., 1691-2, fellow 1693-4, M.A. 1694. baptized in St. Peter's, Chester, 16 Jan., 1671-2, chaplain to William, Earl of Derby, vicar of Middlewich, Cheshire, 1702, until his death 25 Sept., 1718, brother of Thomas same date, and father of Peter 1724. See Rawlinson, vi. 277, xvi. 444.

Cowper, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Chester (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 June, 1688, aged 17; of Over Leigh, Cheshire, admitted to the Inner Temple 1689, M.P. Chester Jan. to July, 1698, buried in St. Peter's, Chester, 18 Aug., 1718, aged 48, brother of John same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Ormerod's Cheshire, i. 375.

Hyde, John, s. J., of Salford, near Manchester, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1688, aged 17; B.A. 1691, M.A. 1694, B.D. 1705; perhaps vicar of Bowden, Cheshire, 1691-1708, rector of Didcot, Berks, 1709-10, and vicar of Steeple Barton, Oxon, 1722-9. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Thickness, John, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1688-9, B.A. 11 Feb., 1692-3, B.C.L. 1695; rector of Farthingho, Northants, 1694 (and perpetual curate of Radstone 1715), until his death, buried there 11 Dec., 1725, aged 55. See Baker's Northants, i. 624; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Slade, Thomas, s. John, of Kingsland, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1688-9, aged 17, B.A. 1692, fellow, M.A. 1695, B.D. 16 July, 1709; died 5 Oct., 1709, buried 8th in college chapel; admon. at Oxford 22 Oct. following. See Gutch, i. 381.

Davie, Samuel, s. J., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1688, aged 17; B.A. 14 March, 1691-2, M.A. 1694, rector of Tattenhall, Cheshire, 1701. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Morris, John, s. David, of Bishops Castle, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1688, aged 16; B.A. from Wadham Coll. 1692, M.A. 1695, rector of Rushbury, Salop, 1692. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Smith, Thomas (Smyth), s. Th., of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1688-9, aged 16, B.A. 1692; incorporated at Cambridge 1695, and M.A. from Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 1695.
Chaddocke, Thomas, s. Th., of Leigh, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1688-9, aged 17; B.A. 1692, vicar of Eccles, co. Lancaster, 1721. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Johnson, Thomas, s. William, of Warington, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1688-9, aged 17; B.A. 1692, vicar of Ribchester, co. Lancaster, 1706. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ogden, Samuel, s. Abraham, of Oldham, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1688, aged 16; B.A. 1692, vicar of Wherwell, Hants, 1698. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bavand, Robert, s. Edw., of Christleton, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1688-9, aged 16; B.A. 1692, M.A. 1699, B. & D. Med. 1710. See Hearne, iii. 21.
Yarborough, Harrington, s. John, of Newark-uponTrent, Notts, D.Med. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 July, 1688, aged 16, B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1692, M.A. 1695.
King, William, s. William, of Cheltenham, co. Gloucester, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1688-9, aged 16; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 1692.
Barret, Michael, s. M [ichael], of Pyecombe, Sussex, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1688-9, aged 15; brother of Serenus and Edward.
Gould, Thomas, s. Henry, of Taddington, Devon, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1688, aged 19; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 1691.
Davenport, Edward, s. E., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1688, aged 16; B.A. 1691, M.A. 1694.
Baguley, William, s. Richard, of Bolton-in-the-Moore, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1688, aged 18.
Wegewood, John, s. William, of Leeke, co. Stafford, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1688, aged 18.
Boulton, Thomas, s. Ralph, of Chester (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 May, 1688, aged 16.

Jeffs, George, s. G., of Westminster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 May, 1689, aged 17; fellow Merton Coll. 1694, B.A. 1692, M.A. 1697; vicar of Diddington, Hunts, 1698, rector of Ibstone, Oxon, 1708, until his death, buried 14 July, 1709, in college chapel; admon. at Oxford 23 Aug., 1709. See St. Paul's S.R. 59; Gutch, i. app. 215; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Bordman, John, s, William, of Chester (city), cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1689, aged 18, B.A. 1693; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 1696, rector of Grappenhall, Cheshire, 1696. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Goldsmith, Charles, s. Matthew, of Isle of Wight, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 May, 1689, aged 17; B.A. 23 Feb., 1692-3, M.A. 1695, rector of Deane, and of Ash, Hants, 1713. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Derwas, Richard, s. Griffin, of Llantrinis, co. Montgomery, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 Nov., 1689, aged 20; B.A. 1693, rector of Meifod, co. Montgomery, 1695. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Carleton, Josias, s. Edward, of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 June, 1689, aged 17; B.A. 11 Feb., 1692-3, B.C.L. 1695, of the Inner Temple 1690. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Powell, George, s. G., of Southampton (town), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 June, 1689, aged 17, B.A. 1693; vicar of South Stoneham, Hants, 1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Brooke, Henry, s. Richard, of Runcorn, Cheshire, bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 July, 1689, aged 17; B.A. 1693, died without issue, brother of Thomas 1685, and Leigh 1696.
Townley, Henry, s. John, of Clitheroe, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1689, aged 18; student of Gray's Inn 1690. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Thelwall, Ambrose, s. John, of Plâs-Coch, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1689, aged 19; student of Inner Temple 1691. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Preston, Peter, s. Edw., of Wemsto (?Wilmslow), Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 June, 1689, aged 18; B.A. from Christ Church 1693.
Baguley, Richard, s. R., of Bolton, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1689, aged 17; B.A. 1693, M.A. 1697.
Purchase, Richard, s. R., of Winslow, Bucks, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. entry 13 Dec., 1689, aged 14; B.A. 1693, M.A. 1698.
Kinge, Charles, s. Ferdinand, of Steeple Aston, Oxon, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 July, 1689, aged 18; B.A. 1693.
Kippax, Peter, s. John, of Colne, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 June, 1689, aged 18; B.A. 1693.
Robinson, William, s. G. (? "Gul."), of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1689, aged 16.
Morecroft, James (Moorcroft), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 Feb., 1689-90, B.A. 1690.

Berkeley, Henry (Barkley), s. Henry, of London, Middlesex, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1690, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1696, and of the Inner Temple, ad eundem 1701, licenced 22 Dec., 1702, to marry Dorothy Middleton, of St. James, Middlesex, spr. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Heton, Thomas, s. James, of Bolton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 Jan., 1690-1, aged 20, B.A. 1694; incorporated at Cambridge 1704, and M.A. from Christ's Coll. 1704, rector of Holwell, Beds, 1701, and Widdial, Herts, 1718. See Rawl. vi. 121, xvii. 251; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Mosley, (Sir) Oswald, s. O(swald), of Manchester, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 Jan., 1690-1, aged 16; of Rolleston, co. Stafford, sheriff 1715, and of Ancoats, co. Lancaster, student of Gray's Inn 1691, created a baronet 18 June, 1720; died 9 June, 1751. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Smethurst, James, s. John, of Abram, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 June, 1690, aged 18, B.A. 19 Jan., 1693-4, fellow, M.A. 7 March, 1698-9, proctor 1708, died 5 March, 1708-9, buried in the college chapel; admon. at Oxford 17 June, 1709. See Gutch, i. 380; & Hearne, ii. 173.

Whalley, Thomas, s. Th., of Clayton, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1690, aged 17, B.A. 1694; fellow Oriel Coll. 1692, M.A. 1699, B.Med. 1708, D.Med. 1711; of Sparth, in the parish of Whalley, co. Lancashire; died unmarried.

Morton, John, s. James, of Billingborough, co. Lincoln, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 May, 1690, aged 16; B.A. 19 Jan., 1693-4, vicar of Weston, co. Lincoln, 1700, rector of Great Oxendon, Northants, 1707. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lever, John, s. R(obert), of Alkrington, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 Jan., 1690-1, aged 15; B.A. 1694, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1700; brother of Robert same date. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Lever, Robert, s. R(obert), of Alkrington, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 Jan., 1690-1, aged 18; B.A. 1694, bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1699; brother of John same date. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Gardiner, James, s. Th., of Middlesex, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 Nov., 1690, aged 16; B.A. 1694, M.A. 1697, rector of St. Michael ad Vincula, London. See Rawl. iii. 154.
Short, Richard, s. R., of Wardington, Oxon, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 July, 1690, aged 18, B.A. 1693; vicar of Deddington, Oxon, 1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Halsted, Charles, s. Laur., of London, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1690-1, aged 16; died 1732; his father keeper of the records in the Tower of London.
Stephens, Stephen, s. Maurice, of Llandynam, co. Montgomery, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 April, 1690, aged 20; B.A. from Christ Church 1694, M.A. 1699.
Stones, Thomas, s. Jos., of Masbrough, co. Derby, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 Nov., 1690; student of Gray's Inn 1693. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Rutton, William, s. Tho., of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 April, 1690, aged 16; B.A. 1693, B.C.L. 1696; perhaps brother of John.
Dennis, Barnes, s. William, of Dodleston, co. Chester, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1690, aged 16; B.A. 1693, M.A. 1696.
Clutton, Richard, s. And., of Chalton, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1690-1, aged 16; B.A. 1694, M.A. 1697.
Langford, William, s. Geo., of Tranmore, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1690, aged 16; B.A. 1692, M.A. 1695.
Smith, Samuel, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 June, 1690, B.A. 1695; perhaps created M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 7 June, 1708.
Pullen, William, s. Henry, of Ribston, Yorks, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 May, 1690, aged 18; B.A. 14 Feb., 1693-4.
Boulton, John, s. Mark, of Staunton, co. Pembroke, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 July, 1690, aged 18; B.A. 1694.
Alcock, John, s. John, of Runcorne. Cheshire, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 April, 1690, aged 20; B.A. 1693.
Plaxton, William, s. G. (? "Gul."), of Wressle, Yorks, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1690, aged 22.
Owen, Robert, s. R., of Llandrinio, co. Montgomery, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 July, 1690, aged 18.
Mather, Thomas, s. J., of Chester (city) gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1690-1, aged 16.

Bracebridge, Samuel, s. Abraham, of Atherston, co. Warwick, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 May, 1691, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1699, bencher 1721, reader 1731, treasurer 1734, M.P. Tamworth 1710-22, died — Nov., 1735, buried at Drayton, co. Leicester. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Richmond, Henry, s. Silv., of Liverpool, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 March, 1691-2, aged 17, B.A. 1695; vicar of Garstang, co. Lancaster, 1698, rector of Hornblotton, Somerset, 1701, and rector of Liverpool (mediety) 1706; brother of Richard 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Russell, Daniel, yst.s. William, of London, equitis. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1691, aged 16; student Inner Temple 1690 (his father probably knighted at Whitehall 20 Oct., 1679, and died 10 June, 1705); see William 1688. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Aldersey, Samuel, s. Thomas, of Spurstow, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1691, aged 16; B.A. 1694, M.A. 1700, of Aldersey and Spurstow, rector of Wigan 1714, until his death in 1742. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Kent, John, s. St., of Sandbach, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1691, aged 21; B.A. 1695, rector of North Cheriton, Somerset, 1725, until his death in 1730; brother of the next-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Moore, John, s. J., of Moore, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 March, 1691-2, aged 16; B.A. 1695, M.A. 1698; perhaps vicar of Holmer 1701, and of Pipe, co. Hereford, 1703. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Egerton, James, s. Ralph, of London, Middlesex, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1691-2, aged 16; B.C.L. 2 July, 1698, rector of Eccleston, co. Lancaster, 1704. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Worrall, Richard, s. R., of Middlewich, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 May, 1691, aged 19, B.A. 14 Feb., 1694-5; died 2 May, 1697, buried in the college chapel. See Gutch, i. 379.
Astry, James, s. James, of Harlington Woodend, Beds, eq. aur. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 Aug., 1691, aged 16; secretary of the comptrollers of the army accounts 1712, buried 19 Oct., 1716.
Arblaster, Edmund, s. Ed., of Longdon. co. Stafford, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 April, 1691, aged 17; of the Inner Temple 1693. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hanmer, William, s. Th(omas), of Ludford, co. Hereford, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1691, aged 17; of Fennes Hall. Salop, died 1724; brother of Job.
Harpur, John, cler fil., born at Oldham, co. Lancaster. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1691-2, aged 17; B.A. 1695; M.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1709.
Meare, John, s. T., of Horton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 May, 1691, aged 16, died 31 July, 1694; brother of Thomas 1694. See Gutch, i. 379.
Kent, Ottiwell (subs.), s. Steph., of Sandbach, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1691, aged 23; brother of the last-named.
Waring, Walter, s. G., (? Gualter), of Shrewsbury, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1691, aged 16, B.A. 1695, M.A. 1698.
Darley, John Nathan, s. J(ohn), of Northill, Cornwall, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1691-2, aged 17; B.A. 1695.
White, Peter, s. Ben., of Lewes, Sussex, doctoris (subscribed med. doc.). Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 April, 1691, aged 18.
Thompson, Richard, s. James, of Tarleton, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1691, aged 17, B.A. 1695.
Arrowsmith, John, s. J., of Grappenhall, Cheshire, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1691-2, aged 18; B.A. 1695.
Bebington, Josiah, s. Ri., of Bickley, co. Chester, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 April, 1691, aged 16; B.A. 1694.
Bowdler, Stephen, s. William, of Hereford (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 April, 1691, aged 15.
Bower, John, s. J., of Burnham, Somerset, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 April, 1691, aged 17.
Lloyd, John, s. Ellis, of Oxford (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1691, aged 16.
Tempest, William, s. J., of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 June, 1691, aged 18.
Royden, John, s. J., London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 July, 1691, aged 17.

Astry, Francis, y.s. James, of London, equitis. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 May, 1692, aged 15, B.A. 1 Feb., 1695-6; fellow of Merton Coll. Feb., 1698-9, M.A. 1702, B. & D.D. 1715, rector of Cornwell, Oxon, 1705, dom. chaplain to Dr. John Robinson (bishop of London) 1714, rector of St. Martin's, Ludgate, London, 1714-17, treasurer of St. Paul's 1715, preb. 1718, rector of Orsett, Essex, 1717, and of St. James's, Garlick, Hythe, 1720, born in London 21 Aug., 1676, died 30 Oct., 1766. See Rawlinson, ii. 242, xvi. 99, & xviii., 236.

Burscough, William, s. John, of Stoke, Surrey, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 July, 1692, aged 16; scholar of Wadham Coll. 1695, B.A. 1696, M.A. 2 March, 1698-9, fellow 1699-1719, B. & D.D. 12 March, 1715-16, rector of Stoke-next-Guildford 1712, chaplain to Lord Carteret when lord lieutenant of Ireland, dean of Lismore 1724-5, bishop of Limerick, Ardfert, and Aghadhoe 1725, buried 3 April, 1755. See Rawlinson, i. 236; & Cotton, i. 170, 388.

Ball, Benedict, s. B., of Whippingham, Isle of Wight, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1692, aged 16; B.A. 1696, M.A. 1699, rector of Connington, Cornwall, 1703, and of Paston, Northants, 1707, his father rector of Huntsham, Devon, 1662, and of Whippingham, aforesaid, 1665. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Syddall, Arnold, s. A., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 April, 1692, aged 17, B.A. 1 Feb., 1695-6; curate and schoolmaster at Gravesend, minister of Aclesbury chapel, St. John's Court, Clerkenwell; ? died in July, 1719. See Rawl. iv. 267.

Tyler, William, s. William, of Kington, co. Hereford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 March, 1692-3, aged 18; B.C.L. from Hart Hall, 1699; vicar of Dilwyn 1699, and rector of Sarnesfield, co. Hereford 1710, canon of Llandaff 1720. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stanley, Thomas, s. William, of Astley, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1692-3, aged 17, B.A. 1696, M.A. 1699, proctor 1708; rector of Begbrooke, Oxon, 1704, and of Middleton Cheney, Northants, 1710. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Frauncis, Benjamin, s. J., of Daventry, Northants, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 May, 1692, aged 16; B.A. 28 Feb., 1695-6, M.A. 1713, vicar of Norton-by-Daventry, 1706, rector of Warmington, co. Warwick, 1726. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lever, Samuel, s. John, of Bolton, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 March, 1692-3, aged 17; B.A. 1696, M.A. 1699, rector of Claughton, co. Lancaster, 1700; brother of Thomas 1690. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Yates, Robert, s. William, of Salop, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 June, 1692, aged 18, B.A. 1696; rector of Wickham Bishops, Essex, 1706. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lloyd, William, s. William, of Halton, Flints, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 Dec., 1692, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1696. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Marshall, Edward, s. Joshua, of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1692, aged 15; student of Inner Temple 1689. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Massie, John, s. Jo., of Chester (city), arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1692, aged 15; perhaps brother of William 1697.
Topp, Edward, s. Rob., of Rochester, Kent, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1692-3, aged 16, B.A. 18 March, 1696-7.
Davies, Thomas, paup. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 Feb., 1692-3; B.A. 1696, M.A. 1699. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Browne, John, s. William, of Middlesex, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1692, aged 16.

Barrell, Edmund, s. Francis, of Rochester, Kent, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1693, aged 16; B.A. 1696, M.A. 1700, born 3 July, 1676, rector of Kingsdown, Kent, 1700, preb. of Norwich 1702, and of Rochester 1705, vicar of Sutton-at-Hone 1706, rector of Fawkham 1712, vicar of Boxley 1720, died 15 March, 1765, brother of Francis 1680. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; & Rawlinson, vi. 141; xvi. 129.

Prichard, Benjamin, cler. fil. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 May, 1693, Elton fellow 1696-1719, B.A. 26 Jan., 1696-7, M.A. 1699, rector of Stonesfield, Oxon, 1705, and of Ratcliffe Stepney, Middlesex, 1719, and of Corringham, Essex, 1725-6; will dated 20 May, 1726, proved 8 Nov., following; brother of William 1698, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Landon, John, s. John, of co. Glamorgan, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 May, 1693, aged 18; B.A. 26 Jan., 1696-7, B. and D.C.L. 1709, minister at Madras about 1706, minister of the chapel at Poplar, and lecturer at St. Dunstan's, Stepney; died 1729. See Gutch MS. 181; & Rawl. v. 451.

Hollier, William, s. William, of Wolverhampton, co. Stafford, paup. (subs. "pleb."). Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1693, aged 16, B.A. 1696; M.A. from Caius Coll., Cambridge, 1710, vicar of Carshalton, Surrey, 1703, until his death 7 March, 1736-7. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Farington, Valentine, s. John, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 May, 1693, aged 16; B.A. 26 Jan., 1696-7; one of these names (2s. of George, of Shawe Hall in Leland, co. Lancaster, gent.), student of Gray's Inn 1695. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Porter, William, s. Joh., of Aston Sandford, Bucks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1693-4, aged 16; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1697; M.A. from Wadham Coll. 1700; perhaps rector of Barnborough, Yorks, 1714. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Symonds, Walter, s. Phil., of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 March, 1693-4, aged 19; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1697, M.A. 17 March, 1700-1; perhaps rector of Munsley, co. Hereford, 1723. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hargreaves, Robert, s. James, of Colne, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 July, 1693, aged 17, B.A. 1697; incorporated at Cambridge 1707, and M.A. from Christ's Coll. 1707; incorporated at Oxford 8 July, 1707.
Shaw, Robert, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 March, 1693, aged 18, B.A. 1696; incorporated at Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1700; rector of Handley, Cheshire, 1703. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rishton, Jeffrey, s. Jeffrey, of co. Montgomery, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 May, 1693, aged 17, B.A. 26 Jan., 1696-7; vicar of Brayton, Yorks, 1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wood, Thomas, s. Tho., of Cirgington, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1693, aged 18, B.A. 1697, M.A. 1700; vicar of Albrighton, Salop, 1701. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ashe, Robert, paup., pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 March, 1693, aged 17; B.A. 1696, M.A. 1699, vicar of Newbottle, Northants, 1699. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Thistlewheat, John, s. Jo., cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1693, aged 16, B.A. 1696; perhaps vicar of Eckington, co. Worcester, 1715-27. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Chadwick, Lewis, s. Lewis, of Rochdale, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 Dec., 1693, aged 17; B.A. 1697, died unmarried.
Garnett, Thomas, paup., (subs.) "pleb." Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 July, 1693, B.A. from New Inn Hall 1698.
Feild, Miles (Field), s. M., cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 July, 1693, aged 18.
Townley, Henry, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 March, 1693, aged 16.
Evans, David, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1693; B.A. 1696.

Rudd, Edward, s. Thom., of Stockton, co. Durham, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1694, aged 17; born 16 June, 1677, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1698-9, M.A. 1702 (incorporated 8 July, 1704), B.D. 1709, D.D. 1717, rector of North Runcton, Norfolk, 1719; his father curate of Stockton 1663, vicar of Norton 1690, and rector of Long Newton, all co. Durham, 1712; died 15 July, 1719, aged 79. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Burches, George, s. Hugh, of Woodchurch, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 June, 1694, aged 18; one of these names was sequestered from Woodchurch 1646, Add. MS. 15,670, Hugh Burches instituted to Woodchurch rectory 9 Oct., 1673-1704. See Foster's Index Eccelesiasticus.

Colly, Jonathan, s. William, of Bruera, co. Chester, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 March, 1694, aged 17; B.A. 1697, M.A. 1700, chantor Christ Church, vicar of Cassington, Oxon, 1718, until he died 27 Oct., 1738. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Gutch, i. 515.

Crowfoot, Robert, s. Rob., of Tatnall, co. Chester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 March, 1694, aged 16; B.A. 1697, M.A. 1700, perhaps rector of Edlesborough, Bucks, 1742-60. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Milne, Thomas, s. James, of Chaldwell, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 March, 1694, aged 17; B.A. 1697, M.A. 1700, rector of Shepton Beauchamp, Somerset, 1723. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Pease, Francis, s. Rob., of Exeter (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1694, aged 20; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 1698; rector of Clyst Wick, Devon, 1705, until his death in 1738. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Wetenhall, Thomas, 1s. Tho., of Namptwich, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 March, 1694, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1700; buried at Nantwich 19 May, 1709. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Alsop, George, s. Nath., of Leicester (town), cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 March, 1694-5, aged 17; B.A. 1698, M.A. 1701, rector of Church Langton, co. Leicester, 1711. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Clayton, John, s. Joh., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 March, 1694-5, aged 19; B.A. 1698, M.A. 1703, rector of Wensley, Yorks, 1703. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Eckersall, Richard, s. Elias, of Foles, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 May, 1694, aged 19; B.A. 20 Jan., 1697-8, M.A. 1700, vicar of Bradwell, Bucks, 1712. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Myddelton, Robert, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1694; perhaps bar.-at-law, of Middle Temple, 1702, as son and heir of Richard, of Shrewsbury, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Varley, Thomas, s. Tho., of Barley, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 March, 1694, aged 19, B.A. 1697; vicar of Great Peatling, co. Leicester, 1706. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Forshall, John, s. William, of Willaston, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1694, aged 16; B.A. 1698, rector of Wallasey (moiety), Cheshire, 1702. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Greene, Thomas, s. Ric., of Poulton, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 March, 1694, aged 16; B.A. 1697, M.A. 1700, rector of Woodchurch, Cheshire, 1705. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Lloyd, Meredith, s. David, of Comber, co. Cardigan, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 March, 1694-5, aged 19; perhaps rector of Llanwenog, co. Cardigan, 1717. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Morris, William, s. "Wm., of Michael Troy," co. Monmouth, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 May, 1694, aged 19; his father rector of Mitchel Troy 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Offley, John, s. Th., of Broughton, co. Stafford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 May, 1694, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1696. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Whishaw, Joseph, s. Joh., of Leighes, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 March, 1694, aged 19; vicar of Audley, co. Stafford, 1703. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Atherton, John, 1s. Ric., of Bewsey, co. Lancaster, eq. aur. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 May, 1694, aged 16; of Gray's Inn 1697. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Meare, Thomas, s. Tho., of Horton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 May, 1694, aged 17; B.A. 20 Jan., 1697-8, M.A. 1700; brother of John 1691.
Arderne, Richard, y.s. Joh., of Harding, Cheshire, equitis. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 March, 1694-5, aged 18; B.A. 1698, M.A. 1 March, 1702-3.
Whitehead, Robert, s. Rob., of Lisle (?), co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 March, 1694, aged 17, B.A. 1697.
Adams, John, s. Tho., of Northwood, Flints, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1694, aged 17; B.A. 1700.
Blackburne, John, s. Tho., of Rockford, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 March, 1694-5, aged 16.
Yates, Bartholomew (subscribes "Bar."), p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 March, 1694-5, B.A. 1698.
Powell, Henry, s. Joh., of Salop, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1694, aged 17.
Fowler, John, cler. fil. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 March, 1694, B.A. 1698.

Collins, Richard (Collings), s. Ric., of Hereford (city), cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1695, aged 16; B.A. 1699, chaplain to Mary, Countess Dowager of Fauconbergh, vicar of Burham, Kent, 1701, of Meopham 1707, and rector of Crayford 1711, naval chaplain at Chatham. See Rawlinson, i. 377, v. 495; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Seward, William, s. William, of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 Aug., 1695, aged 15, B.A. 1699; M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1710; vicar of Vowchurch, co. Hereford, 1704, rector of St. David's, alias Llanthewy-skerrid, co. Monmouth, 1717, and rector of Llangunider, co. Brecon, 1729. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Newcome, Henry, s. Hen., of Middleton, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1695-6, aged 17; B.A. 1699, M.A. 1702, rector of Yelford, Oxon, 1708, and of Didcote, als. Dudcote, Berks, 1711; brother of Nicholas 1712. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hall, Samuel, s. Sam., of Eardisley, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 April, 1695, aged 18; B.A. 18 Feb., 1698-9, B.C.L. 10 March, 1726-7, vicar of Tytherington, co. Gloucester, 1709, and of Eardisley 1727. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Clerk, John, s. John, of Hereford (city), cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1695, aged 14; B.A. 1699, M.A. 1706, vicar of Brinsop, co. Hereford, 1707, and of Yazor 1707, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rodd, Thomas, s. Hump., of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 July, 1695, aged 17; B.A. 1699, rector of St. Peter with vicarage of St. Owen, Hereford (city), 1700-46. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Barkham, (Sir) Edward, s. Robert, of Rowlright, Oxon, Bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1695, aged 15; of Wainflete, co. Lincoln, 3rd baronet, died 13 Feb., 1711.
Osbaldeston, Alexander, s. Edw., of Lancaster (town), arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 May, 1695, aged 16; student of Gray's Inn 1695. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Sunderland, John, s. Joh., of Croston, Yorks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 May, 1695, aged 19, B.A. 17 Jan., 1698-9; brother of William same date.
Sunderland, William, s. Joh., of Croston, Yorks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 May, 1695, aged 16, B.A. 17 Jan., 1698-9; brother of John same date.
Assheton, Thomas (or Ashelton), s. Th., of Aishley, co. Chester, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1695-6, aged 16. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Glasiour, Thomas, s. Tho., of Chester, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 April, 1695, aged 17. See Ormerod's Cheshire, ii. 386.
Hampton, John, s. Joh., of Bouldsworth, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1695-6, aged 18.
Walley, Charles, s. Charles, of Chester (city), arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1695, aged 18.

Skerrett, Ralph, s. Joh., of Shavington, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1696, aged 15, B.A. 1700; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1706, D.D. 1720; rector of Oakley, Suffolk, 1713, domestic chaplain to earl of Grantham, rector of St. Peter-le-Poor, Bread Street, London, 1720, vicar of Greenwich, Kent, 1725. See Rawl. iii. 170, and xxi. 105; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Whiteside, John, s. George, of Kirkham, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 May, 1696, aged 16, B.A. 18 Jan., 1699-1700, M.A. 1704, chaplain of Christ Church, keeper of the Ashmolean museum 1714, until his death 12 Oct., 1729, buried in the cathedral, will and admon. at Oxford 5 Jan., 1730. See Rawl. vi. 310; Gutch, i. 478.

Wrighte, Nathan, s. Nath., of Leicester (town), arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 1 April, 1696, aged 16, B.A. 1699, M.A. 1702; student of Lincoln's Inn 1698; rector of Taplow 1703, and of Farnham, (both) Bucks, 1703, canon of Norwich 1703; died 15 Aug., 1721; brother of Thomas 1708. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Brooke, Legh, s. Richard, of Norton, Cheshire, baronet. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 April, 1696, aged 16; B.A. 1699, M.A. 1706, rector of Tarporley, Cheshire, 1715, of Selham, Sussex, 1718, and of Cottingham, Northants, 1739, brother of Thomas 1685, and of Henry 1689. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Goodwin, John, s. Step., of Horley, Oxon, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 May, 1696, aged 16; B.A. from New Inn Hall 26 Feb., 1699-1700, M.A. 1705, rector of Paglesham, Essex, 1706-52; brother of Matthew 1701, and of Stephen 1694. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Glegge, Edward, s. William, of Grange, Cheshire (co. Leicester in Mat. Reg.), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 May, 1696, aged 16, of Caldey Grange, Cheshire; student of Gray's Inn 1696; died 4 Aug., 1714. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Baylye, Robert, s. William, of Marlborough, Wilts, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 April, 1696, aged 17; B.A. from Wadham Coll. 1699, M.A. 1702, one of these names vicar of Studland, Dorset, 1712. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Whip, William, s. Sim., of Burnley, co. Lancaster, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 April, 1696, aged 18, B.A. 8 Feb., 1699-1700; M.A. from New Inn Hall 1705; vicar of Leighton Buzzard, Beds, 1712. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Harding, Brian, s. Hen., of Drynton, co. Stafford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 May, 1696, aged 20; B.A. 18 Jan., 1699-1700, rector of CoombeD'Abitot, co. Worcester, 1731. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Collier, Philip (Collyere), s. David, of Coxwell, Berks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1696-7, aged 16; B.A. 1700 (as Collier), vicar of Great Coxwell, Berks, 1724. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Nanfan, Giles, s. Giles, of Eastworth, co. Hereford, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 May, 1696, aged 17; B.A. 3 Feb., 1699-1700, rector of Birts Morton, co. Worcester, 1703. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Fleetwood, Thomas, s. Tho., of Prescot, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll, matric. 15 Oct., 1696, aged 16; B.A. 1700, M.A. 1702, rector of Huyton, co. Lancaster, 1708. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Marten, Thomas, s. Tho., of St. Gennys, Cornwall, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1696-7, aged 18; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 1700; perhaps rector of Halwell, Devon, 1705. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stanhope, Thomas, s. Joh., of Elvaston, co. Derby, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 July, 1696, aged 16; of Elvaston, M.P. co. Derby, 1702-5; died 10 April, 1730. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Sayce, James, s. James, of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1696, aged 17; one James Seys ded a fellow of that college 13 Sept., 1736. See Gent's Mag. 1736, p. 553.
Baker, Thomas, s. John, of Ledbury, co. Hereford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1696-7, aged 17, B.A. from Christ Church 1700; M.A. from Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1709.
Jones, Isaiah, s. Amos, of Gloucester city, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 April, 1696, aged 16; rector of Bacton 1705, and of Ganarew, co. Hereford, 1721. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ferrers, William, s. John, of Preston, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 July, 1696, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1705. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Tucker, William, s. William, of South Petherton, Somerset, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 Oct., 1696, aged 21; vicar of Cobham, Surrey, 1700. See Rawl. vi. 48.
Hatton, Joseph s. Ric, of Dean Church, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 March, 1696-7, aged 18; B.A. 1700, M.A. 1703.
Smith, William, s. Joh., of Astewmond, co. Leicester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 May, 1696, aged 19, B.A. 18 Jan., 1699-1700.
Malham, Francis, s. Hen., of Colne, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1696, aged 17, B.A. 1699.
Daxon, Edward, s. Edw., of Chester (city), cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1696, aged 16; B.A. 1699.
Oakes, Stephen, s. Joh., of Somerford, co. Leicester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 May, 1696, aged 18.
Winterborne, Robert, s. Rob., of Hanney, Berks, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 May, 1696, aged 18.
Church, William, s. Ric., of Tunstall, Salop, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 Dec., 1696, aged 17.

Massie, Richard (Middleton), s. Edw., of Rostherne, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 March, 1697, aged 15; a non-juror, one of the keepers of the Ashmolean, hon. fellow college of physicians 1726, practiced at Wisbeach and Stepney, D.Med. Aberdeen 7 March, 1720, F.S.A. 1718, and secretary 1725-6, F.R.S. 1712; died at Rostherne 29 March, 1743, aged 65, buried in the chancel there. See Rawl. v. 452; Munk's Roll, ii. 93; & Hearne, iii. 174.

Dod, Pierce, s. J(ohn), of Hackney, Middlesex, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1697-8, aged 14, B.A. 1701; fellow All Souls' Coll., M.A. 1705, B.Med, 22 March, 1710-11, D.Med. 1714, of Lincoln's Inn 1706, F.R.S. 1730, fellow College of Physicians 1720, physician to St. Bartholmew's Hospital 1725, until his death 18 Aug., 1754, brother of John same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; Rawlinson, iv. 276; Munk's Roll, ii. 70; & D.N.B.

Goodwin, Benjamin, s. Math., of Bloxam, Oxon, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 May, 1697, aged 17; B.A. 4 Feb., 1700-1, M.A. 26 Feb., 1703-4, rector of Stockerston, co. Leicester, 1704, vicar of Draughton, Northants, 1705. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Whishaw, Hugh, s. Hug., of Middlewich, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 March, 1697, aged 17, B.A. 1700; vicar of Madeley 1723, and of Diddlebury, (both) Salop, 1726, rector of Dinton, co. Lincoln, 1731. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Young, William, s. G. (? Gul.), of Oxford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1697, aged 18, B.A. 1701; one of these names vicar of St. Nicholasat-Wade in Thanet 1701, and of Chislet, (both) Kent, 1705. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Mainewaring, Roger, s. John, of Peel, Cheshire, bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1697, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1700; died 11 June, 1707; brother of Thomas same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Bosworth, Henry, s. Henry, of Kemsey, co. Worcester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1697, aged 16; B.A. 1701, M.A. 1707, a student of the Middle Temple 1698. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Mainewaring, (Sir) Thomas, 1s. John, of Peel, Cheshire, bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1697, aged 16; 3rd bart., born at Peel 7 Aug., 1681; died 20 Sept., 1726; brother of Roger same date.
Duckworth, James, s. Joh., of Haslington, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 March, 1697, aged 18; B.A. 1700, vicar of Weaverham, Cheshire, 1712. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Woodhouse, Thomas, s. Sam., of Shobden, co. Hereford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1697-8, aged 19, B.A. 1701; vicar of Cliftonupon-Teme, co. Worcester, 1707. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Dod, John, s. Jo., of Hackney, Middlesex, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1697-8, aged 16; B.A. 1701, M.A. 1704, B. & D.Med. 1711, brother of Peirce same date.
Pollexfen, Reed, s. Nic., of 'Waden,' Surrey, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 Aug., 1697, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1699. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Boulton, Richard, s. Ralph, of Chester (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1697, aged 20; a physician. See Rawlinson, i. 169 and iv. 82; & D.N.B.
Fox, John, s. Tho., of Lemster, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 July, 1697, aged 17; B.A. 11 Feb., 1700-1, M.A. 1703, B. and D.Med. 1714.
Massie, William, s. Joh., of Coddington, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1697, aged 16; died s.p. 1729; perhaps brother of John 1692.
Barnston, Trafford, s. Trafford, of Corrington, co. Chester, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 April, 1697, aged 15. See Ormerod's Cheshire, ii. 747.
Grenville, William (Greenvill), 1s. Christopher, of Preston, co. Lancaster, equitis. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 July, 1697, aged 15.
Holbrook, John, s. John, of Bromyard, co. Hereford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 June, 1697, aged 17; B.A. 1701.
Osborne, Farley, s. William, of Stoke Priors, co. Hereford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1697-8, aged 16.
Ball, Hugh, s. H., of Worsewall, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 March, 1697-8, aged 17; B.A. 1701.
Lloyd, Walter, s. Dav., of Llanbadarn, co. Cardigan, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 July, 1697, aged 19.
Hetherington, Isaiah, s. William, of London, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1697-8, aged 18.
Page, Matthew, s. M., of Wilcote, Oxon, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1697, aged 15.
Shipton, John, s. James, of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 May, 1697, aged 15.

Bunce, John, s. Ste(phen), of Deal, Kent, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 Dec., 1698, aged 15; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 3 Feb., 1702-3; incorp. at Cambridge 1727, and M.A. from Trinity Hall 1727, vicar of Brenzett, Kent, 1707, and of Newington-juxta-Hythe 1726, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Leigh, Thomas, s. Geor., of Leigh, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 April, 1698, aged 16; B.A. 1701, fellow, M.A. 1704, incorporated at Cambridge 1707, rector of Stoke Bruern, Northants, 1719, until his death; buried there 5 Sept., 1751. See Rawl. MS. & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Earle, Peter, s. John, of Warrington, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 April, 1698, aged 18; B.A. 1701, M.A. 1704, rector of Grappenhall, Cheshire, 1706, until his death 15 Nov., 1708. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Foster's Lancashire Collection.

Shakerley, George, y.s. Jeff., of Gwersilt, co. Denbigh, equitis. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1698. aged 15; of Holme and Gwersillt, student of Middle Temple 1709; died 2 Feb., 1756; brother of Peter 1667. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Withers, Richard, s. Jos., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 April, 1698, aged 18, B.A. 1701; M.A. from Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1719; vicar of Westbourne, Sussex, 1705. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Meare, Thomas, s. Joh., of Oxford (city), doctoris. Brasenose Coll. matric. 16 April, 1698, aged 14; perhaps died 10 July, 1714, and buried 12th in the college chapel; brother of John same date. See Gutch, i. 381.

Hall, John, s. Tho., of Eccles, Lancashire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Feb., 1698-9, aged 17; B.A. 1702; one of these names rector of Babington, Cheshire, 1708. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Lowe, Joseph, s. Geo., of Chelford, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1698, aged 19; B.A. 12 Feb., 1701-2, M.A. 1706, vicar of Prestbury, Cheshire, 1722. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Oulton, Charles, s. Edw., of Chester (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1698, aged 15; B.A. 6 Feb., 1702-3, vicar of St. John Baptist, Chester, 1710. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Vaughan, Richard, s. Joh., of Nantmill, co. Radnor, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1698, aged 18, B.A. 24 March, 1701-2; vicar of Norton, Radnor, 1705. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Meare, John, s. Joh., of Oxford (city), doctoris. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 April, 1698, aged 14; B.A. 1701, M.A. 1704; perhaps canon of Wells 1703-10; brother of Thomas same date.
Adney, George, s. G., of Westnott, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1698, aged 16; B.A. 1702, vicar of Ditton Priors, Salop, 1704. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Cooper, Richard, s. R., of Stanton, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 April, 1698, aged 19; B.A. 1701, vicar of Madeley, Salop, 1706. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Oldfeld, Leftwich, s. L., of Leftwich, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1698, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1698. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hulse, Thomas, s. T., of Middlewich, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 April, 1698, aged 17; student of Gray's Inn 1698. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Chestroe, Nathaniel, s. N., of Cheltenham, co. Gloucester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 June, 1698, aged 18; B.A. 12 Feb., 1701-2.
Eyre, Charles, of Ewelme, Oxon, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1698-9, aged 16; B.A. from Christ Church, 1702, M.A. 1705.
Symonds, John, s. William, of Rosebury (Bosbury), co. Hereford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1698, aged 18, B.A. 1701.
Chambers, Joseph, s. William, of Warrington, co. Lancaster, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 April, 1698, aged 18; B.A. 1701.
Mercer, John, s. William, of Chester (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 April, 1698, aged 15; B.A. 1701, M.A. 1704.
Seddon, Roger, s. Tho., of Farnworth, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1698, aged 18, B.A. 1701.
Moss, Adam, s. Ric., of Hilton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 April, 1698, aged 17, B.A. 1701.
Pott, Edward, s. Joh., of Morton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 March, 1698-9, aged 19, B.A. 1702.
Slater, John, s. William, of Shaw, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 March, 1698-9, aged 16, B.A. 1702.
Wilbraham, Thomas, s. Ralph, of Dorfold, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 July, 1698, aged 16.
Grosvenor, "Fulke," s. F, of London, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Feb., 1698-9, aged 15.

Watson, Joseph, s. J., of Eccles, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 March, 1699-1700, aged 18, B.A. 1703, M.A. 1707, B. and D.D. 1720; born at Eccles 14 June, 1681, lecturer of St. Botolph, Bishopsgate, 1711, rector of St. Stephen, Walbrook, and of St. Bennet Sherehog, London, 1719, chaplain to William Withers, lord mayor of London 1708; died in Hatton Garden 7 Dec., 1737, buried in Walbrook. See Rawl. v. 45, and xix. 259; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Finch, James, s. Rob., of Wigan, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 May, 1699, aged 18; B.A. 6 Feb., 1702-3, fellow, M.A. 1705, rector of Selham, Sussex, 1716, and of Stepney, alias Radcliffe, Middlesex, 17 Nov., 1718, until his death 8 Dec. following, buried 12th. See Gutch, i. 380; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Robertshaw, Benjamin, s. B., of Burnley, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1699, aged 18; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 11 March, 1702-3; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1706; incorporated 12 June, 1708, vicar of Penn 1716, and rector of Agmondesham, Bucks, 1728. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Biddulph, Michael, s. Anthony, of Ledbury, co. Hereford, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1699-1700, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1711, bencher 1740, treasurer 1750, died in 1758, probably brother of Robert 1699. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Smith, Henry (Smyth), s. Henry, of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 March, 1699-1700, aged 19, B.A. 1703; M.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1713; rector of Aylton 1709, and vicar of Dormington, co. Hereford, 1713. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Clayton, Ralph, s. Ric., of Disley, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1699-1700, aged 18, B.A. 1703; M.A. from Hart Hall 1709, vicar of Kilsby, Northants, 1720, and of Bierton, Bucks, 1729. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Pargiter, Samuel, s. Tho., of London, gent. Brasenose Coll. matric. 21 June, 1699, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 5 March, 1704-5, vicar of Earls Barton, Northants, 1717. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Whittell, William, s. William, of Aymstree, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 Aug., 1699, aged 20; rector of Stretford, co. Hereford, 1711; brother of Thomas 1709. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Booth, Robert, s. George, of Chester (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 Dec., 1699, aged 19; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1704, grandson of Sir John. See Foster's Gray's Inn Admissions.
Hall, Timothy, s. Christopher, of Stalming, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 Dec., 1699, aged 18; B.A. 1703, vicar of Bolton, co. Lancaster, 1714. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Fewtrell, Thomas, s. Ric., of Apcot, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1699-1700, aged 18; B.A. 1703, rector of West Zoyland, Somerset, 1710. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Leche, Henry, s. John, of Stretton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 June, 1699, aged 16; B.A. 1703, M.A. 1707, fellow 1707; brother of Thomas 1696. See Hearne, ii. 18.
Gartside, Adam, s. Rob., of Prestwich, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 Oct., 1699, aged 16; B.A. 1703, fellow, M.A. 1708; died 1 Jan., 1725-6. See Gutch, i. 380.
Iles, Henry, s. Joh., of Hereford city, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1699-1700, aged 18; B.A. 1703, rector of Ganarew, co. Hereford, 1720. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Dale, Thomas, s. Rog., of Denton, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 June, 1699, aged 16; B.A. 1703, brother of Joseph 1699, and of William 1705.
Tilden, Samuel, s. Sam., of Cranbrooke, Kent, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1699-1700, aged 18, B.A. 1703; died 30 March, 1704. See Gutch, i. 379.
Twittey, John, s. J., of Wadd (Wath), Yorks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1699, aged 16, B.A. 9 March, 1702-3, as Jonathan; brother of William.
Howell, John, s. Tho., of Ablington, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 May, 1699, aged 16; B.A. 22 Feb., 1702-3, M.A. 1705.
Jaye, Nathan, s. Francis, of Berkeley, co. Gloucester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1699-1700, aged 18; B.A. 1703.
Aynscough, Radley, s. Tho., of Bowden, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 March, 1699-1700, aged 18; B.A. 1703.
Hesketh, William, s. G. (? Gul.), of Carrington, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 March, 1699-1700, aged 17.
Lead better, Robert, s. Pet., of Darlton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1699, aged 18.
Bayly, James, s. J., of Bitton, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 Oct., 1699, aged 18; B.A. 1703.
Hartill, Henry, s. Sam., of Birmingham, co. Warwick, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 July, 1699, aged 17.
Blathwayt, William, of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1699, aged 15.

Newcome, Daniel, s. D., of Saltford, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 Feb., 1700-1, aged 16; B.A. 20 Feb., 1704-5; M.A. from Peter House, Cambridge, 1719, vicar of Hunmanby, Yorks, 1708, dean of Gloucester 1730, until his death 3 March, 1758. See Rawl. v. 467; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Dod, Thomas, s. George, of Highfield, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Sept., 1700, aged 18; B.A. 1704, fellow 1706, M.A. 1707, proctor 1715, rector of Spittalfields 1718-20, died 27 July, 1727, brother of George same date. See Rawlinson, v. 485.

Garencieres, Theophilus, s. Dudley, of Chester (city), cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 Feb., 1700-1, aged 18; B.A. 1704, M.A. 1728, vicar of Scarborough, Yorks, 1721, and of Stainton in Cleveland 1731 until his death in Oct., 1750. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Taylor, John, s. Joh., of Colton, co. Stafford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1700-1, aged 16, B.A. 1704, M.A. 1707; rector of Colton 1708, until his death 7 Sept., 1735. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bateman, James, s. Geor., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1700-1, aged 17; B.A. 1704, one of these names rector of Hollingbourne, Kent, 1724. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Seward, Jacob, s. Isa., of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1700-1, aged 17, B.A. 1704; vicar of Weston Zoyland, Somerset, 1726 (as James), until his death in 1728. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Dod, George, s. George, of Highfield, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Sept., 1700, aged 19; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1708, brother of Thomas same date. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Hesketh, William, s. Barn., of Meoles, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1700-1, aged 19; B.A. 1703; vicar of Syston, co. Leicester, 21 Oct., 1709. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Alsop, William, s. Nath., of Langton, co. Leicester, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 May, 1700, aged 16; B.A. 18 Jan., 1703-4, M.A. 1706. See Nichols' Leicester, ii. 694*; iv. 802.
Fleetwood, Edward (Fletewood), s. Ric., of Rossall, co. Lancaster, gent. (after arm.). Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 June, 1700, aged 17; o. Rossall, born 13 Jan., 1682; living 1722.
Morgan, Thomas, s. Joh., of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 March, 1700-1, aged 16; B.A. 1704, vicar of Dewsall, co. Hereford, 1718. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Gabell, Thomas, s. Tho., of Bloxham, Oxon, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1700-1, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1707. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Nevile, Brian, s. B., of Lincoln (city), arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 June, 1700, aged 17; died 19 July, 1702, buried in college chapel. See Gutch, i. 379.
Warburton, George, s. Tho., of Winnington, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 June, 1700, aged 18; of Winnington; died s.p. 29 Aug., 1728.
Jones, Theophilus, s. Dav., of "Stretton Sugwas" (4to.), co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1700-1, aged 16; B.A. 1706.
Worthington, John, s. William, of Weeton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 April, 1700, aged 18, B.A. 1704.
Shaw, Edward, s. Silvester, of Eye, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 March, 1700-1, aged 18, B.A. 1704.
Hope, Richard, s. Foulk, of "Nitchells." co. Stafford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 May, 1700, aged 18.
Matthews, Thomas, s. T., of Hereford (city), pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 March, 1700-1, aged 18.
Spire, John, s. Anth., of Brightwell, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 Jan., 1700-1, aged 18.

Bradshaigh, Thomas, s. Roger, of Haigh, co. Lancaster, baronet. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 June, 1701, aged 17; B.A. 22 Jan., 1704-5, M.A. 1707, born 29 Oct., 1664, educated at Manchester Grammar School, rector of Stratford, Suffolk, 1710, and of Langham, Essex, 1714, chaplain in ordinary to the king. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Rawlinson, ii. 252 and xvi. 246.

Smith, John, s. Jo., of Clifton, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1701-2, aged 18, B.A. 1705; (perhaps M.A. by diploma 17 Dec., 1714, nephew to Dr. Radcliffe), bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1722; buried in Temple church 15 Dec., 1753, brother of James, B.A. and M.A. 1714. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Fazakerley, Nicholas, s. William, of Prescot, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 March, 1701-2, aged 17; bar.-at-law of the Middle Temple 1707, of the Inner Temple 1716, and of Lincoln's Inn 1734, treasurer 1747, M.P. Preston Jan., 1732, until his death in Feb., 1767. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Hayward, Pengry, s. Sam., of Gloucester city, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1701-2, aged 16; B.A. 1705, M.A. 1708, incorporated at Cambridge 1735, in orders; treasurer of the infirmary, James Street, Westminster, died 27 Aug., 1758, buried in Westminster Abbey 4 Sept. See Chester's West. Abbey Reg. 392.

Newcome, Peter, s. Peter, of Aldenham, Herts, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 Dec., 1701, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 1705; M.A. from Brasenose Coll. 1708; vicar of Wellow, Hants, 1715, and rector of Batcombe, Somerset, 1728, and of Shenley, Herts, 1742, until his death 31 May, 1744. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Barton, Henry, s. Phil., of Thornebury, co. Hereford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 Dec., 1701, aged 16; B.A. from Christ Church 1705; M.A. from Brasenose Coll. 1708, one of these names rector of Bredicot and of Churchill, co. Worcester, 1739. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Radclyffe, Edward, s. Rob., of Ordsall, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 March, 1701-2, aged 16, B.A. 1705, fellow, M.A. 1708; baptised at Withenshaw, 1 Dec., 1685; fellow of All Souls', according to Foster's Lancashire Collection; admon. at Oxford 6 Dec., 1723.

Veale, John, s. J., of Whineyheys, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1701-2, aged 16, B.A. 7 March, 1705-6, M.A. 1708; rector of Pirton 1713, of Croome D'Abitot 1725, and of Grafton Flyford, co. Worcester, 1729. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Badham, Benjamin, s. Joh., of Pembridge, co. Hereford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 April, 1701, aged 24: one of these names vicar of Lyonshall, co. Hereford, 1737. See Foster's Index Ec.
Howorth, John, s. Tho., of Bowchurch, co. Hereford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 June, 1701, aged 17; B.A. 22 March, 1704-5; vicar of Boughrood, co. Radnor, 1718. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Taylor, Jeremiah, s. Benj., of Madeley, Salop, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 May, 1701, aged 17, B.A. 20 Feb., 1704-5, M.A. 1709; vicar of Madeley 1709. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Percivall, John, s. Pet., of Lyme, Cheshire, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 April, 1701, aged 26; B.A. 1704, M.A. 1709, rector of Ardley, Oxon, 1707. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Jones, Thomas, s. John, of Chester city, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 May, 1701, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1708. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Smethurst, Ralph, s. Ric., of Watkinton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1701-2, aged 19, B.A. 1705, M.A. 1708.
Smith, George, s. Ric., of Burnley, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 April, 1701, aged 18, B.A. 22 Jan., 1704-5.
Calkin, William, s. William, of Waberton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1701-2, aged 17; B.A. 1705.
Flanner, Thomas, s. Tho., of Wisbech, Isle of Ely, co. Cambridge, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 June, 1701, aged 17.
Greene, Amos, s. William, of Bromyard, co. Hereford, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1701-2, aged 17; B.A. 1705.
Baylye, Thomas, s. William, of Marlborough, Wilts, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Feb., 1701-2, aged 15; B.A. 1705.
Woodland, Joseph, s. Joh., of Hinckley, co. Leicester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 Feb., 1701-2, aged 18.

Whishaw, Thomas, s. Hugh, of Middlewich, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 July, 1702, aged 17, B.A. 1706; M.A. from Trinity Hall, Cambridge, 1713; vicar of Lalford, Beds, 1713, rector of Great Munden, Herts, 1718, precentor 1722, and canon of Hereford 1723, canon of Sarum 1727, and of Winchester 1739-56. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Prichard, Herbert, s. Benjamin, of Ledbury, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 July, 1702, aged 15; B.A. 1706, M.A. 17 Feb., 1709-10, rector of Spitalfields Stepney, Middlesex, 1728-9, and rector of Christ Church, Spitalfields, 1729-30; brother of Benjamin 1693. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Horton, Thomas, s. "Joshua" (4to.), of Chester city, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 May, 1702, aged 17; of Chadderton, co. Lancaster, justice of the peace, governor of the Isle of Man for Lord Derby; born 4 May, 1685, died at Manchester 18 March, 1757. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.

Robinson, John, s. Henry, of Farrington, Dorset, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 May, 1702, aged 17; B.A. 1706; incorporated at Cambridge 1721, and M.A. from Clare Coll. 1721, rector of East Stoke 1708, and vicar of Combe Keynes, Dorset, 1721. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hale, William, s. Ric., of "Waden," Surrey, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 Sept., 1702, aged 16; M.P. Bramber, Jan., 1709, St. Albans, 1713 (unseated April, 1714), 1715, until his death 2 Oct., 1717. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Le(i)gh, Calverley, s. Ric., of Lyme, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1702, aged 16; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 24 Jan., 1705-6, M.A. 1710, B. Med. 1716, D.Med. 1720; admon. (as D.C.L.) at Oxford 20 May, 1727.

Rainstorp, Walter, s. Hugh, of Bristol (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 March, 1702-3, aged 16; B.A. 1706, M.A. 1709, rector of Crompton Greenfield, Dorset, 1724. until his death 9 Oct., 1769. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Griffiths, Charles, s. Rob., of Hereford (city), pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 March, 1702-3, aged 17; B.A. 1706 (as Griffith), rector of Llangunider, co. Brecon, 1706. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ryland, John, s. Peter, of Manchester, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 Feb., 1702-3, aged 17, B.A. 1706; vicar of Hawkesbury, co. Gloucester 1712. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Clerke, William, s. (William), of Thame, Oxon, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1702-3, aged 16; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1706, M.A. 1709. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Fieldhouse, John, s. Edward, of Dunstan, co. Stafford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 1 July, 1702, aged 18; B.A. 1706, vicar of Shawbury, Salop, 1720. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Eccles, Thomas, s. Edward, of Adlington, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 1 July, 1702, aged 18; a student of Middle Temple 1703. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Davies, Robert, s. Robert, of Llannerch, co. Denbigh. arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 June, 1702, aged 16; of Gwysaney, Flints, a Welsh antiquary, died 22 May, 1728.
Giffard, Thomas, s. William, of London, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 June, 1702, aged 19; B.A. 1706, rector of Rushall, Wilts, 1708. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Remington, Thomas, s. Tho., of South Cerney, co. Gloucester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 March, 1702, aged 17, B.A. 7 March, 1705-6, M.A. 1710.
Jordan, John, s. Sam., of Ludford, Salop, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 March, 1702-3, aged 16; B.A. 1706, M.A. 29 Jan., 1713-14.
Bordman, John (Boardman), s. Thomas, of Great Lever, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 June, 1702, aged 21; B.A. 1706.
Lovatt, John, s. J., of Clayton, co. Stafford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1702-3, aged 18; B.A. 1706.
Hooper, Richard (Hoper), s. Richard, (4to.), of London, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 May, 1702, aged 17.

Browne, Thomas, s. Francis, of London, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 July, 1703, aged 16; bar.at-law, Inner Temple, 1709, bencher 1738, 'carried out of the Temple to be buried elsewhere 30 Nov., 1743,' his father is described also as of Nether Legh, Cheshire. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Simpson, William, s. Thomas, of Chester (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 July, 1703, aged 16, B.A. 1707, M.A. 1711, B. and D.D. 1731; born at Chester 25 March, 1687, rector of St. Georgein-the-East (Wapping) 1729. See Rawl. iii. 42, and xix. 35; & Foster's Index Eccl.
