Willis, Thomas, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 May, 1703; student of Middle Temple 1703 (as son and heir of Martin, of Monck Hall, Eccles, co. Lancaster, esq., deceased); died aged about 40. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Harris, Thomas, s. T., of Bristol, co. Gloucester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 Feb., 1703-4, aged 16; B.A. 1707; M.A. from Balliol Coll. 1710, rector of Flyford Flavell, co. Worcester, 1717. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Powlet, Francis, s. Fran., of Inglesfield, Berks, "March-Nep." Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 March, 1703-4, aged 15 (subs. Baronis fil.); grandson of John, 5th Marquis of Winchester; perhaps brother of Norton.
Hopley, Randolph, s. "Randolphi", of Malpas, Chester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 May, 1703, aged 18; B.A. 20 Jan., 1706-7, fellow, M.A. 1713; died 18 July, 1727. See Gutch, i. 380.
Goulborne, Samuel, s. Geor., of Warrington, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 March, 1703-4, aged 18; B.A. 1707, fellow, M.A. 1710; died 27 April, 1718. See Gutch, i. 380.
Bootle, Caryll, s. Thomas, of Melling, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 May, 1703, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1703. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Colclough, Cæsar, s. Cæsar, of Delfhouse, co. Stafford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 July, 1703, aged 16; of the Inner Temple 1704. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Martin, William, s. Joh., of Newton (cum Seales, 4to.), co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 March, 1703-4, aged 19; B.A. 1707 M.A. 1710.
Lawrence, William, s. G. (? "Gul."), of Bewdley, co. Worcester, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 April, 1703, aged 18; B.A. 20 March, 1706-7.
Worden, John, s. James, of Ormskirk, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 March, 1703-4, aged 16, B.A. 1707.
Leigh, Thomas, s. Alex., of Northenden, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Nov., 1703, aged 17, B.A. 1707.

Egerton, (Sir) Holland, s. John, of Heaton, co. Lancaster, Bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1704, aged 15; 4th Bart., died at Heaton 25 April, 1730; brother of Edward, Ralph, and Thomas. See Foster's Baronetage.

Bevan, Arthur, s. Zachariah, of Langhorne, co. Carmarthen, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1704-5, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1712, M. P. Carmarthen 1727-41. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Hayward, John, s. J., of Tetnor, co. Stafford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1704, aged 17; B.A. 1707, M.A. 1710; perhaps vicar of Rostherne, Cheshire, 1727. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wall, George, s. John, of Roston, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 March, 1704-5, aged 16, B.A. 1708.
Poyer, Thomas, s. T., of Haverfordwest, co. Pembroke, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1704, aged 19.
Carrington, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Ince, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 July, 1704, aged 16.

Capell, Gamaliel, s. Edward, of Stanton, Suffolk, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 June, 1705, aged 18; B.A. 1706, M.A. 1709, rector of Carleton-cumWillingham, co. Cambridge, 1716, and of Stanton, All Saints, 1720, and St. John 1720, died 1758. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hallings, William, s. William (4to.), of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1705, aged 18; B.A. 1708; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1726, rector of Evesbach, co. Hereford, 1714; brother of the last-named. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Bethell, Samuel, s. William, of Hereford (city), p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1705, aged 17; B.A. 1709, rector of Dynedor, co. Hereford, 1731, died 9th and buried 13 Nov., 1766. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Wright, Charles, s. C., of Leighton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 March, 1705-6, aged 18, B.A. 1709; died 14 Aug., 1710, buried in the college chapel. See Gutch, i. 380.
Watkins, Thomas, s. William, of Aston, co. Hereford, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 March, 1705-6, aged 17; rector of Knill, co. Hereford, 1717-30, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Grace, Isaac, s. John, of Childwall, co. Lancaster, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 March, 1705-6, aged 24; rector of Saxmundham, Suffolk, 1716. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ellis, John, s. John, of Wilsford, Wilts, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 June, 1705, aged 16; B.A. 8 Feb., 1708-9, M.A. 1712, B. and D.D. 1730.
Wilbraham, Richard, s. Randle, of Nantwich, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1705, aged 16; died 6 Feb., 1709, buried at Astbury.
Robinson, Samuel, s. Joh., of Rabbington (? Bebington), Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 March, 1705-6, aged 17.
Plymley, John, s. J., of Wolverhampton, co. Stafford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 Oct., 1705, aged 18; B.A. 1709.
Eden, Thomas, s. Rob., of Newton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 March, 1705-6, aged 18; B.A. 1709.
Taylor, Richard, s. Sam., of Chester (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1705, aged 16, B.A. 1709.
Smith, James (Smyth), s. James, of Hereford (city), p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 March, 1705-6, aged 18.
Wright, John, s. J., of Burrington, co. Hereford, p.p. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 March, 1705-6, aged 17.
Broade, Edward, s. E., of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1705, aged 15.
Featley, John, s. Henry, of Newark, Notts, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 May, 1705, aged 21.

Arrowsmith, Edward, s. Joh., of Warrington, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1706-7, aged 17; B.A. 1710, M.A. 1715, vicar of Mitcham, Surrey, 1717, rector of St. Olave, Hart Street, 1720, president of the Corporation of the London Clergy 1737. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Rawlinson iii. 165; xvi. 94.

Dicken, Samuel, s. Jos., of Birmingham, co. Warwick, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1706-7, aged 16, B.A. 1710; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1719, rector of Hemingford Abbots 1714-48, and vicar of Hemingford Grey 1724-44, and rector of Houghton with Witton 1744-8, all in Hunts. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rode, William, s. Christopher, of Bickley, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1706-7, aged 18, B.A. 1710; student of Middle Temple 1708; died 24 May, 1712; buried in the college chapel. See Gutch, i. 380; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Lytton, Lytton, ("Tho." in Mat. Reg.), 1s. George Strode, of Gresford (Guersfelt), co. Denbigh, equitis. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1706-7, aged 18; of Knebworth, Herts; assumed the surname of Lytton in lieu of Strode, and died 1710.

Leftwiche, Nath, s. Tho., of Chester city, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1706-7, aged 19, B.A. from New Coll. 1 Feb., 1711-12; incorporated at Cambridge 1719, and M.A. from St. John's Coll. (Cambridge) 1719.

Walmsley, Henry, s. Henry, of Mellar, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 Feb., 1706-7, aged 18; B.A. 1710; vicar of East Preston 1716, and rector of Combes, (both) Sussex, 1749. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Burton, John, s. 'Josuae,' of Swinbrook, Oxon, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Feb., 1706-7, aged 17; B.A. 1712, rector of Rousham, Oxon, 1717, and vicar of Chesterton 1720-7. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rice, John, s. David, of Carmarthen (town), cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 June, 1706, aged 18, B.A. 1710; perhaps rector of Letterston 1716, and of Herbranston, co. Pembroke, 1730. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Shallcross, John, s. John, of Stockport, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1706, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1707; died 1709. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Shurtt, John, s. Ric., of Stowgalson (Stogursey), Somerset, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1706-7, aged 19; B.A. 1710.
Hancock, John, s. Nat., of Norwich (city), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 March, 1706-7, aged 19; B.A. 20 April, 1708.
Daniell, Thomas, s. John, of Runcorn, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 Feb., 1706-7, aged 18; B.A. 1710.
Andrews, Thomas, s. 'Elias,' of Chipping Norton, Oxon, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1706-7, aged 17.
Ward, Thomas, s. Alex., of Shenston, co. Stafford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 Dec., 1706, aged 22.

Foot, Walter, s. George, of Madley, co. Hereford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 May, 1707, aged 18, B.A. 6 Feb., 1710-11; perhaps M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1719, and vicar of Chalgrave, Beds, 1712-43, and of Harlington, Beds, 1718-50, rector of Halstead, Kent, 1725-42, rector of Hockliffe, Beds, 1743-50. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Egerton, Ralph, y.s. John, of Wrinehill, co. Stafford, bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Nov., 1707, aged 16; B.A. 23 March, 1712-13, a student of the Inner Temple 1708, died unmarried 3 April, 1732; brother of Edward same date and of Thomas. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Woodhouse, Robert, s. Fran., of Shobden, co. Hereford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1707, aged 17, B.A. 1711; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1720; rector of Hopesay, Salop, 1716, and of Hammeringham, co. Lincoln, 1720. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Egerton, Edward, y.s. John, of Wrinehill, co. Stafford, Bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Nov., 1707, aged 17; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 1714, of Harleston, co. Stafford; died 9 May, 1727; brother of Ralph, same date, and of Thomas, and of Holland, 1704.

Cattell, Thomas, s. William, of Warwick (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 May, 1707, aged 17; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 19 March, 1710-11; M.A. from New Inn Hall 1715, chaplain of Christ's College in Manchester. See Rawlinson, iii. 344.

Armitstead, William, s. Hugh, of Clapham, Yorks, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 July, 1707, aged 21; B.A. 1711, vicar of Shottswell, co. Warwick, 1713, and of Chalcombe, Northants, 1717, buried there 28 Aug., 1737. See Foster's Index Ecc.

Egerton, John, s. Nic. (John in 4to.), of Instow, Devon, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 June, 1707, aged 17; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 1711, M.A. 16 Feb., 1713-14, rector of East Allington, Devon, 1717. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Cartwright, John, s. John, of Shrewsbury, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 June, 1707, aged 16; B.A. 1711, M.A. 1714, vicar of Middlewich, Cheshire, 1719, rector of Chester St. Martin, 1724. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rothwell, James, s. Ralph, of Bolton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 June, 1707, aged 18, B.A. 1711; vicar of Dean, co. Lancaster, 1713, until his death 16 May, 1766. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Wrighte, Thomas, y.s. Nathan, of Leicester (town), equitis. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 March, 1707-8, aged 16; died 3 July, 1710, buried in the college chapel; brother of Nathan 1696. See Gutch, i. 377.
Liptrott, William, s. James, of Nuneaton, co. Warwick, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Nov., 1707, aged 17; B.A. 1711 (as John), vicar of Wolvey, co. Warwick, 1719. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Armitsteade, James, s. Robert, of Mursley, Bucks, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 April, 1707, aged 17; B.A. 1710 (as 'Armestead'), rector of Ashingdon, Essex, 1712. See Foster's Index Ecc.
Ashley, Joseph (or Aishley), s. Joseph, of London, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 June, 1707, aged 16; B.A. 1711, M.A. 29 Jan., 1713-14.
Lomax, Thomas, s. James, of Hilton-Over, co. Lancaster, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 March, 1707-8, aged 16; B.A. 28 Feb., 1711-12.
Clough, Thomas, s. Henry, of Kennion, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1707-8, aged 19; B.A. 1711.
Clegg, Ralph, s. Ellis, of Bolton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 April, 1707, aged 19; B.A. 1710.

Vaughan, Thomas, s. Griffith, of Coppenhall, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 June, 1708, aged 18, B.A. 1712; M.A. from Catherine Hall, Cambridge, 1719, vicar of Pawlett, Somerset, 1723-6, and of Eccles, co. Lancaster, 1726. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Entwisle, William, s. Bertin, of Wigan, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1708, aged 17; B.A. 1711, M.A. 1716, rector of Badsworth, Yorks, 1717, until his death unmarried, 7 Oct., 1732. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Drake, Humphrey, s. Hump., of Englefield, Berks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 July, 1708, aged 15; B.A. 1712. [Addenda: licenced (v.g.) 8 July, 1691, to marry Catherine, daughter of Christopher Rigby, of Cosgrave, Northants.]
Dicken, William, s. Tho., of Drayton, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 Nov., 1708, aged 18; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1712, rector of Sheinton, Salop, 1714. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Entwisle, Richard, s. Richard, of Foxholes, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 May, 1708, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1696; died unmarried. See Foster's Inns of Court Registers.
Lawson, William, s. James, of Kirkham, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1708, aged 15; B.A. 1711, rector of Radcliffe, co. Lancaster, 1724. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Bullicocke, Christopher, s. Rob., of 'Cod,' co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 March, 1708, aged 18; B.A. 1712, as Bulcock, called Bullocke in Mat. Reg., also Bullcock.
Brooke, Richard, s. Thomas, of Runcorn, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1708, aged 16; died in 1720, father of Sir Richard, 4th baronet, and brother of Henry 1714.
Cary, Thomas, s. Richard, of Chiswick, Middlesex, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 July, 1708, aged 15; a student of the Middle Temple 1708. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Burton, Philip, s. P., of London, Middlesex, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1708, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1721. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Bedford, William, s. Edw., of Oxford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1708-9, aged 17; B.A. 1712. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dodd, Ralph, s. Ralph, of Little Budworth, co. Chester, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 July, 1708, aged 18; B.A. 1712, M.A. 1717.
Bolton, Samuel, s. Rog., of Upholland, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1708, aged 18; B.A. 1711, M.A. 1714.
Malbon, Thomas, s. Tho., of Bridgvil, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1708, aged 19; B.A. 1711, as Edward.
Sidebottom, William (Sydebotham), s. Rob., of Cheadle, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1708, aged 15.
Orme, William, s. Pet., of Height, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1708, aged 18; B.A. 1711.
Byron, John, s. J., of Rannell, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 March, 1708, aged 18; B.A. 1711.
Delves, William, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 April, 1708; B.A. 1 Feb., 1711-12 (as John Delve), M.A. 1714.
Sharples, George, s. Geor., of Liverpool, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1708, aged 16.
Clark, John, s. John, of Chelmsford, Essex, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 May, 1708, aged 16; B.A. 1712.

Russell, John, s. Joh., of London, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Feb., 1709-10, aged 16, B.A. 1713; fellow Merton Coll. 1715, M.A. 1717, chaplain to White Kennett, bishop of Peterborough, canon of Peterborough 1720-62, and of Lincoln 1724-62, rector of Fiskerton, co. Lincoln, 1722-50. See Rawl, i. 214b., xviii. 236, 333b., and xxi. 93. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Skerratt, John, s. Edw., of Newall, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 April, 1709, aged 17, B.A. 1712; M.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1727; vicar of Kingston-by-Taunton, Somerset, 1716, rector of Brereton, Cheshire, 1731, and vicar of Biddulph, co. Staff., 1733. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Sutton, John, s. J., of Grappenhall, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1709-10, aged 18, B.A. 1713; perhaps M.A. from Catherine Hall, Cambridge, 1717, vicar of Ampney Crucis, co. Gloucester, 1719, and canon of Bristol 1723, &c. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Payne, Robert (Paine), s. Sam., of par. S. Michael at Bristol, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 May, 1709, aged 14; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 13 Feb., 1712-13, M.A. 1716, rector of Chew Stoke, 1719, and of Norton Malreward, Somerset, 1721. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Clerke, James, s. John, of Madeley, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 March, 1709-10, aged 16; B.A. 1713, M.A. 1719, vicar of Frome Bishops 1719, rector of Hugonis Chapelry and of Sollers Hope, co. Hereford, 1720. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Leigh, Ralph, s. Rob., of Blackroad, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1709-10, aged 19; B.A. 1714; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1722, rector of Ightham, Kent, 1725, until his death 28 May, 1760. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Sidebottom, Samuel, s. Sam., of Oldham, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 22 June, 1709, aged 19, B.A. 4 Feb., 1712-13; M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1718; rector of Middleton, co. Lancaster, 1714. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Wright, Thomas, s. Ric., of Crunton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 April, 1709, aged 19, B.A. 1712, M.A. 20 Feb., 1717-18, B. and D.D. 1731; rector of Christ Church, Spitalfields, Middlesex, 1730. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, John, s. Tho., of Llanwrst, co. Denbigh, pp. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 Feb., 1709-10, aged 18; perhaps B.A. from Christ Church 1 March, 1713-14; possibly a student of Inner Temple 1710. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Symonds, William (Simmonds), s. John, of Knowle, co. Warwick, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 July, 1709, aged 17, B.A. 1713, M.A. 1716; rector of Elmdon, co. Warwick, 1720. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Brereton, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Northope, Flints, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 April, 1709, aged 18; B.A. 1712, dramatic writer, died in Feb., 1722. See Rawlinson, i. 380; & D.N.B.
Bushell, William, s. William, of Preston, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 June, 1709, aged 17; a student of the Inner Temple 1711. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hughson, John, s. (James, of Burton ?) Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1709, aged 16; B.A. 1713, fellow, M.A. 1716; died 9 Feb., 1720. See Gutch, i. 380.
Comberbach, Roger, s. Roger, of Chester (city), arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1709, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1721. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Tatlocke, John, s. James, of Wigan, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 June, 1709, aged 17, B.A. 13 Feb., 1712-13, M.A. 1716.
Betham, William, s. G. (? 'Gul.'), of Coplington, Bucks, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 June, 1709, aged 18; B.A. 21 Jan., 1712-13.
Norcross, John, s. William, of Barton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 4 June, 1709, aged 16; B.A. 1713, M.A. 1716.
Brownell, William, s. Nathaniel, of Halsall, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 Dec., 1709, aged 19; B.A. 1711.
Short, John, s. Pet., of Tenterden, Kent, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 July, 1709, aged 17, B.A. 1710, as Shurtt.
Webster, Wriggan, s. Tho., of Frodsham, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 April, 1709, aged 17, B.A. 1712.
Acton, Thomas, s. Ro., of Keele, co. Stafford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 March, 1709, aged 19; B.A. 1712.
Morgan, Edward, s. Edward, of Wigan, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1709, aged 17.
Cooke, John, s. Joh., of Bristol, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 July, 1709, aged 15.

Kilner, James, s. Tho., of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 March, 1710-11, aged 17; B.A. 1714, M.A. 1717; born at St. Dunstan-in-theEast 30 March, 1694, educated at Westminster school, chaplain to 3rd troop of horse guards 1718, chaplain in ordinary to the king, served Southgate chapel 1723, rector of Daglingworth, co. Gloucester, 1723-9, and of Lexden St. Leonard, Essex, 1729, vicar of Tolleshunt Darcy, Essex, 1741, until his death 26 Nov., 1769. See Rawl. vi. 314, xvii. 382; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Leyborn, Robert, s. Ant., of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 March, 1710-11, aged 17 (student of Christ Church 1712); B.A. 19 Jan., 1714-15, fellow, M.A. 1717, proctor 1723, B. & D.D. 1731; principal of St. Alban Hall 1736-59, and rector of St. Dunstan, Stepney, 1729, and of St. Anne, Limehouse, 1730, until his death at Bath 13 May, 1759. See Al. West. 257; Gutch MS. 184; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Brooke, Thomas, s. Benedict, of Brereton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1710, aged 17, B.A. 1714; incorp. at Cambridge 1719, M.A. from Cath. Hall 1719, LL.D. 1732, rector of Nantwich 1720-58, dean of Chester 1732-58, rector of Doddleston, Cheshire, 1738-58, born at Brereton 27 Feb., 1692, and died 23 Dec., 1758, brother of John 1714. See Rawlinson, v. 436, xvi. 315.

Bullock, Thomas, s. Edw., of co. Hereford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1710-11, aged 17, B.A. 1714, M.A. 1717; B.D. & D.D. from Christ Church 1736, vicar of Mitcham, Surrey, 1721, chaplain to the Bishop of Norwich, rector of Ashby with Thirne 1724, and of North Creyk, Norfolk, 1727, and dean of Norwich 2 Aug., 1739, until his death 30 May, 1760. See Rawlinson, v. 130.

Lloyd, John, s. Ric., of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 March, 1710, aged 17 (subs. as Floyd); B.A. 29 Jan., 1713-4, M.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1725; perhaps rector of Berrington, Salop, 1716-43, another vicar of Boughrood, Radnor, 1719-20, and rector of Cregina, Radnor, 1724-53. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Egerton, Thomas, s. John, of Wrinehill, Stafford, Bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 July 1710, aged 15; B.A. 16 March, 1714-15, M.A. 1717, rector of Warrington 1719-23, of Sefton, co. Lancaster, 1723, and of Cheadle, Cheshire, 1723 until his death 1762; brother of Edward and Ralph. See Earwaker, i. 223; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Wilbraham, Roger, s. Randle, of Astbury, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1710, aged 16; of Nantwich, student of Lincoln's Inn 1711; ancestor of the Delamere branch; died in Sept., 1754; brother of Randle 1711. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Juxon, Benjamin (Jouxson), s. B(enjamin), of Ubly, Somerset, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 March, 1710-11, aged 16; B.A. 1714; perhaps vicar of East Budleigh, Devon, 1719; his father rector of Ubley 1693-1716, as 'Dieux Jouxson.' See Weaver, 296.

Tringham, Thomas, s. Tho., of All Saints, Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 May, 1710, aged 17, B.A. 18 Feb., 1713-14; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1723; master of the free school at Lucton, co. Hereford; died 30 March, 1759.

Smith, James, s. Joshua, of Colne, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1710-11, aged 18, B.A. 15 Oct., 1714; perhaps M.A. by diploma 7 Dec., 1714, nephew of Dr. Radcliffe and perhaps brother of John, B.A. Brasenose Coll. 1705.

Case, Thomas, s. Joh., of Huyton, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1710-11, aged 18; B.A. 1714, vicar of Great Wakering, Essex, 1716-61, and rector of Wickford 1731-61. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Hammond, George, s. John, of Gawsworth, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 Nov., 1710, aged 16; B.A. 1714, M.A. 1720, rector of Syresham, Northants, 1723, until his death in 1760. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hulton, Edward, s. James, of Kedmorson (? Todmorden), co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1710-11, aged 17; B.A. 15 Oct., 1714; M.A. from Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 1722. See Man. S.R. iii. 233.

Rode, James, s. Christopher, of Stafford (town), gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1710-11, aged 17; B.A. 1714, M.A. 1717, vicar of Biddulph, co. Stafford, 1720, as Rhode. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Currer, Haworth, s. Henry, of Kildwick, Yorks, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 May, 1710, aged 18; of Kildwick, baptized there 26 Jan., 1690, and died there 13 April, 1744. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.

Cawley, Thomas, s. Th., of Shotwick, co. Chester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 1 April, 1710, aged 17; B.A. 1713, M.A. 1717, vicar of Newingtonjuxta-Hythe, Kent, 1725-6. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Entwisle, Robert, s. Richard, of Foxholes, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1710-11, aged 17; of Foxholes, J.P. co. Lancaster; died 1778. See Foster's Lancashire Collection.
Birch, John (Burch), s. James, of Manchester, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1710-11, aged 17, B.A. 1714; M.A. from New Coll., 1715. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Floyd, John, s. Ric., of Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 March, 1710, aged 16; B.A. 29 Jan., 1713-14, as Lloyd, which see. See also Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Gray, Bennett, s. And., of "Mottram," Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1710-11, aged 16; B.A. 22 Feb., 1714-15, called Benj. in Mat. Reg.
Lamprey, James, s. Ric., of Priors Marston, co. Warwick, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1710, aged 15; B.A. from Christ Church 3 March, 1714-15.
Parker, Robert, s. R., of Extwistle, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1710, aged 17. See Foster's Lancashire Collection.
Crosby, Henry, s. Rob., of Chester (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 March, 1710-11, aged 18; B.A. 1714, M.A. 1717.
Day, Richard, s. R(ichard), of Long Marston, co. Gloucester, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 March, 1710, aged 17.
Hyde, John, s. Newman, of Stockport, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 March, 1710, aged 19; B.A. 1713.
Bateson, Robert, s. (William), of Boreton, co. Gloucester, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 Nov., 1710, aged 18.
Rowley, John, s. William, of Lawton, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 May, 1710, aged 19, B.A. 1714.
Southby, Thomas, s. T., of Cumnor, Berks, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 March, 1710-11, aged 15, B.A. 1714.
Wade, Cuthbert, s. Cuth., of Kilfoy, Ireland, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 Nov., 1710, aged 17, B.A. 1714.
Bankes, John, s. J., of Liverpool, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 March, 171011, aged 18.
Poole, John, s. Hug., of Bebbington, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 24 March, 1710-11, aged 17.
Lloyd, Asshton, s. Hump., of Northop, Flints, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 Jan., 1710-11, aged 15.
Herle, Francis, s. Charles, of Westham, Essex, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 18 Nov., 1710, aged 18.

Goldsmith, Jonathan (Gouldsmyth), s. John, of Nantwich, Cheshire, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1711-12, aged 17; B.A. 1715, M.A. 1718, B. and D.Med. 1724, fellow college of physicians 1726, F.R.S. 1730; licenced 14 Aug., 1729, to marry Elizabeth Atherton, of Atherton, co. Lancaster, widow, baptised at Nantwich 8 May, 1694; died in Norfolk Street, Strand, 12 (or 17) April, 1732. See Munk's Roll, ii. 95; & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Wilbraham, Randle, s. R(andle), of Astbury, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1711, aged 16, D.C.L. by diploma 14 April, 1761, then deputy steward of the university; of Rode, Cheshire, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1718, treasurer 1754, vice-chamberlain of Cheshire, M.P. Newcastle-under-Lyme (Nov.) 1740-7, Appleby 1747-54, Newton, co. Lancaster, 1754-68; died 3 Dec., 1770; brother of Roger 1710. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Thomas, John, s. Joh., of St. Bridgetts, Chester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 April, 1711, aged 17, B.A. 23 June, 1715; M.A. from Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, 1721; baptised at St. Bridgets aforesaid 29 Oct., 1692 (his father a saddler); curate of Chrisleton, Cheshire, and taught school there, minister of Ince 1724, chaplain on board of man of war; buried at St. Catherine's in the Tower 10 May, 1744. See Rawl. vi. p. 284b.

Shelbery, Corbet, s. Joh., of Gatcombe, isle of Wight, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 June, 1711, aged 17, B.A. 1716; vicar of Middleton alias Long Parish, Hants, 1722, rector of St. Lawrence, and of Chale, Isle of Wight, 1734. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Onions, James, s. Tho., of Badger, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 May, 1711, aged 18; B.A. from New Inn Hall 25 Feb., 1714-15; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1722, rector of Foston, co. Leicester, 1736. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Tipping, Moses, s. Rob., of Roston, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 29 Feb., 1711-12, aged 19, died 25 June, 1714, buried in the college chapel, See Gutch, i. 380.
Newcome, Nic, s. Henr., of Middleton, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1711-12, aged 16; B.A. 1715 (as Thomas); brother of Henry 1696.
Assheton, Richard, s. Robert, of Rochdale, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 11 Feb., 1711-12, aged 17; B.A. 1715, M.A. 13 Jan., 1718-19.
Holme, Edward, s. Tho., of Rochdale, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 May, 1711, aged 17; B.A. 19 Jan., 1714-15, M.A. 1718 as Edmund.
Cholmondeley, Robert, s. R., of Great Budworth, Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 April, 1711, aged 15. See Hearne, iii. 153.
Stock, Nathan, s. Phil., of Rochdale, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Feb., 1711-12, aged 18, B.A. 1715, M.A. 1718.
Foot, Walter, s. Walter, of " D Herford," pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 27 June, 1711, aged 16; B.A. 1715. See preceding entry.
Barrett, Miles, s. Joh., of 'Dean par., Lancashire,' pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 July, 1711, aged 18; B.A. 1715.
Sedgwick, Edward, s. E., of Lymm, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1711-12, aged 17; B.A. 1715.
Yate, Thomas, s. Sam., of Alkerton, Oxon, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 Jan., 1711-12, aged 15, B.A. 1715.
Whitehead, Henry, s. H., of Padiham, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 July, 1711, aged 18.
Snooke, Henry, s. H., of Ringmere, Sussex, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 1 Aug., 1711, aged 17.
Alcock, William, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 14 July, 1711; B.A. 23 June, 1715.

Grosvenor, (Sir) Robert, s. Thomas, of Eccleston, Cheshire, Bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 Oct., 1712, aged 16; 6th bart., student of Inner Temple 1716, M.P. Chester (Jan.), 1733, until his death 1 Aug., 1755; brother of Thomas same date. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Blackburne, John, s. J(onathan), of Orford, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 April, 1712, aged 17; a student of the Middle Temple 1712, lord of the manor of Warrington by purchase, died 20 Dec., 1786. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Lancashire Collection.

Harris, Thomas, cler. fil. Brasenose Coll., matric. 2 Dec., 1712, B.A. 1716, M.A. 1722 (s. Nathaniel, 1683), vicar of Frindsbury, Kent, 1725, rector of Stourmouth 1726, and Gravesend 1728, vicar of Northfleet 1726. See Rawl. xvii. 208, and Foster's Index Eccl.

Allanson, Edward (Alanson), s. Edward, of Winwick, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 July, 1712, aged 16; B.A. 1716, M.A. 1719, rector of Clifton Keynes 1723, and of Hardmead, Bucks, 1729. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Rawlinson, v. 46.

Chatfield, Thomas, s. (Thomas), of Balcomb, Sussex, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 Jan., 1712-13, aged 18; B.A. 1716, rector of Southease, Sussex, 1723, and of Balcombe 1730, his father instituted to Balcombe 1693. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Whitcombe, William, s. William, of Adderley, Salop, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1712-13, aged 19, B.A. 1716; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1734; rector of Stockton-onTeme, co. Worcester, 1718. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Foxlowe, Joseph, s. William, of Prestbury, Cheshire, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1712-13, aged 17; rector of Burrough-on-the-Hill, co. Leicester, 1721. See Foster's Index Eccl.; for account of his father see Earwaker, ii. 208.

Grosvenor, (Sir) Thomas, s. Tho., of Eccleston, Cheshire, Bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 Oct., 1712, aged 17; 5th bart., M.P. Chester 1727, until his death 31 Jan., 1733-4; brother of Robert same date. See Foster's Peerage.

Clutton, Ralph, s. Owen, of Newton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 6 Nov., 1712, aged 16; B.A. 1717, M.A. 1725, vicar of Portslade, Sussex, 1722, and rector of Horsted Keynes 1738. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Bateson, Thomas, s. Christopher, of Chipping, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 May, 1712, aged 20, B.A. 23 Feb., 1715-16; vicar of Cosby, co. Leicester, 1718, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Assheton, Ralph, s. R., of Blackburn, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1712-13, aged 16; of Downham and Cuerdale, co. Lancaster, died 21 Sept., 1729. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Earnshaw, John, s. James, of Holme, Yorks. pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1712, aged 17; perhaps B.A. 1 June, 1716 (as Edward), vicar of Glossop, co. Derby, 1721. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pigott, Francis, s. George, of Manchester, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1712, aged 17; B.A. 1716, M.A. 1719, vicar of Gillingham, Kent, 1730. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Woodward, Robert, s. Rob., of Newport Pagnell, Bucks, cler.; entered of Merton Coll. 4 March, 1701-2, aged "9"; and of Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 July, 1712, aged 17, B.A. 1715, M.A. 1718.
Brooke, William (Brock), s. William, of Chester (city). Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1712-13, aged 17; a student of the Inner Temple 1713. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Dod, John, s. William, of Malpas, co. Chester, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 March, 1712-3, aged 17; B.A. 1716, M.A. 1721. See Ormerod's Cheshire, ii. 673, 682, 688.
France, John, s. Edw., of Alston, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1712-13, aged 18; died 15 Sept., 1716. See Gutch, i. 380.
Pollard, Timothy, s. Ric., of Walton, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 May, 1712, aged 18; B.A. 23 Feb., 1715-16, as Thomas.
Egerton, Philip, s. Philip, of Oulton, co. Chester, Doctoris. Brasenose Coll., matric. 30 April, 1712, aged 16, of Oulton, etc., died s.p.
Earnshaw, James, s. James, of Holme, Yorks, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 10 Oct., 1712, aged 18; perhaps B.A. 1 June, 1716, as Edward.
Evans, Robert, s. Evan, of Bala, co. Merioneth, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 Oct., 1712, aged 17; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 1716.
Feilden, Thomas, s. Rob., of Harwood Magna, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 March, 1712-13, aged 22, B.A. 1716.
Hayward, Thomas, s. Tho., of Warwick town, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 March, 1712-13, aged 17, B.A. 1716, M.A. 1719.
Phillips, James, s. Laur., of Whitchurch, Salop, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 23 March, 1712-13, aged 19; B.A. 1716.
Heginbottom, John, s. Ralph, of Hyde, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 March, 1712-13, aged 17; B.A. 1716.
Hanson, William, s. James, of Mabberly, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 July, 1712, aged 19; B.A. 1716.
Oldes, Thomas, s. T., of Drayton-Bassett, co. Stafford, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 31 March, 1712, aged 18.
Robertshaw, Thomas, s. Ben., of Burnley, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 12 April, 1712, aged 18.
Wells, John, s. Jo., of Hungerford, Berks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 3 March, 1712-13. aged 17.

Brooke, Henry, s. Thomas, of Chester (city), bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 March, 1713-14, aged 17, B.A. 1717, M.A. 1720; fellow All Souls' Coll., B.C.L. 1723, D.C.L. 1727, regius professor civil law 1736, until his death 24 March, 1742, brother of Richard 1708. See Rawlinson, iii. 438.

Sneyd, Ralph, s. R., of Bickton, co. Stafford, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-14, aged 17, B.A. 1717, M.A. 1720, B. and D.D. 1742; vicar of Colwich, co. Stafford, 1724, rector of Sible Hedingham, Essex, 1733, until his death about 23 Dec., 1758. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Richmond, Sylvester, s. Rich., of Walton, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 May, 1713, aged 17; B.A. 1 March, 1716-17, M.A. 1719; vicar of Walton-on-the-Hill 1720, and rector 1722, until his death in 1768. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Stawell, Thomas, s. Th., of "Luckea" (Luccombe), Somerset, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 13 Oct., 1713, aged 17, B.A. 1717; rector of Hawkridge, Somerset, 1728; his father rector of Luccombe 1687-1732, as Thomas Stowell, M.A. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Drinkwater, Peter, s. Nath., of Grappenhall, co. Chester, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1713, aged 19; B.A. 1716, M.A. 1720, vicar of Raunds & Oundle, Northants, 1724, chaplain to the Duke of Manchester. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Jenk(e)s, Samuel, cler. fil. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-14; B.A. 1717, M.A. 1720, vicar of Audlem, Cheshire, 1731, and of Ellesmere, Salop, 1732, until his death 20 March, 1672. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Halsted, William, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-14, B.A. 1717, M.A. 1720; one of these names vicar of Thornborough, Bucks, 1724, another rector of Farnborough, Hants, 1724. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Denson, Robert, s. Ric., of Wervin (? Tarvin), Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 March, 1713-14, aged 21; B.A. 10 March, 1717-18, vicar of Backford, Cheshire, 1720. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Parker, Thomas, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-14, B.A. 1717; possibly student of Middle Temple 1708, as son and heir of Edward, of Browsholme, Yorks, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Fenton, Thomas, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-14, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1721, as 2s. of John, of Newcastle, co. Stafford, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Springett, Nathaniel, s. Fran., of Bradfield, Berks, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 Dec., 1713, aged 16, B.A. 1717, as Matthew; brother of Francis 1704.
Cooke, William, s. John, of Nash, Bucks, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 May, 1713, aged 16; B.A. 20 March, 1716-17. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Prichard, Samuel, s. Benjamin, of Ledbury, co. Hereford, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 Feb., 1713-14, aged 16; brother of William 1698, etc.
Taylor, Edward, s. Zach., of Wigan, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 May, 1713, aged 16, B.A. 27 Feb., 1716-17, M.A. 1722.
Foxley, John, s. J., of Prescott, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 25 Feb., 1713-14, aged 16; B.A. 1717, M.A. 1721.
Hyde, Robert, s. Joh., of Bolton, co. Lancaster, cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1713, aged 22; B.A. 4 March, 1717-18.
Oldfeild, Robert, s. Joh., of Manchester, co. Lancaster, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 1 March, 1713-14, aged 17; B.A. 1717.
Pierpoint, Nathaniel, s. Ed., of Prescot, co. Lancaster, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 28 March, 1713, aged 16; B.A. 1716.
Wright, Thomas, s. Joh., of Stepney, Middx., cler. Brasenose Coll., matric. 5 March, 1713-14, aged 17, B.A. 1717.
Meare, Peter, s. Thomas, of Horton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1713, aged 16; B.A. 1717.
Davenport, William, s. Geo., of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-14, aged 18; B.A. 1717.
Davies, Roger, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 March, 1713; died 5 March, 1715. See Davids, Gutch, i. 380.
Worth, Thomas, s. John, of Luccombe, Somerset, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 19 Feb., 1713-14, aged 17.
Egerton, John, s. Peter, of Chester city, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-14, aged 18.
Longfield, James, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-4; B.A. 1717.
Liptrott, Edmund, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 March, 1713-4.
Hughes, Thomas, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 9 March, 1713-14.

Ramsden, Thomas, s. Tho., of Halifax, Yorks, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 June, 1714, aged 17; of Crowstone and High Framley, Yorks, high sheriff 1726, died 29 July, 1727, aged 28; his son assumed the additional name of Hawksworth. See Foster's Yorkshire Collection.

Cotton, (Sir) Robert Salusbury, s. Thomas, of St. Margaret's, Westminster, Bart. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 July, 1714, aged 19; 3rd baronet, M.P. Cheshire 1727-34, Lostwithiel 1741-7, died 27 Aug., 1748. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Green, Thomas, s. Thomas, of Birmingham, co. Warwick, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1714, aged 17; B.A. 1718, M.A. 1721, rector of St. Martin's, Birmingham, 1723-8. See Foster's Index Eccl. and Gents. Mag. 1766, p. 47.
Bird, John, s. William, of All Saints, Hereford (city), pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 16 Dec., 1714, aged 18, B.A. 1718; incorp. at Cambridge 1723, M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge. 1723.
Longworth, Edward, s. Ri., of Garstang, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1714, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1714. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Brooke, John, s. Benedict, of Brereton, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 21 July, 1714, aged 17; B.A. 1718, M.A. 1721, brother of Thomas 1710.
Booth, Thomas, s. Laur., of Sandwitch (Sandbach), Cheshire, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 17 July, 1714, aged 19.
Butler, Thomas, s. Alexander, of Kirtland, co. Lancaster, arm. Brasenose Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1714, aged 18.
Kitchen, Jonathan, s. Rob., of Mobberley, Cheshire, pleb. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 June, 1714, aged 18.
Wright, William, s. Hen., of London, gent. Brasenose Coll., matric. 8 June, 1714, aged 16.
